title: Noise TOP
text: Noise TOP generates images with noise patterns. Parameters: Type menu (Random, Turbulence, Sparse, Worley, Alligator, Fractal, Curl), Amplitude, Frequency, Seed, Monochrome, Animate Scope, Period. Use it as a displacement or pattern source. The Fractal type adds octaves for fractal noise. Connect its output into a Displace TOP or a Feedback/Level loop.

title: Movie File In TOP
text: Movie File In TOP reads a movie or image file into TOPs. Parameters: File (par.file, NOT filename), Play, Speed, Audio Rate, Cue, Trim Start/End, Caching, Preload. Python class is moviefileinTOP_Class. Common mistake: the parameter identifier is 'file' not 'filename'. Set in Python with op('moviein1').par.file = 'path'. Use 'Caching' = Automatic / Always / RAM / GPU to control memory.

title: Blur TOP
text: Blur TOP blurs an image. Parameters: Pre-Sharpen, Kernel menu (Box, Gaussian, Radial, etc.), Width, Height, Level, Aspect. Use before a Threshold TOP for a glow. For a simple Gaussian blur set Kernel to Gaussian and raise Level. Combine with a Level TOP for bloom.

title: Level TOP
text: Level TOP adjusts brightness, contrast and gamma of an image. Parameters: Pre, Post, Gamma, Black, White, Mono, Invert. Essential in feedback loops to decay/offset the previous frame before recombining in a Feedback TOP. Post controls the overall brightness floor used for trails.

title: Feedback TOP
text: Feedback TOP feeds its own previous output back as an input, creating trails and feedback loops. Wire a Level TOP (or transform) into Feedback's second input so each frame is offset/decayed before re-combining. Combine with Noise TOP and a Threshold TOP for classic generative feedback. Set 'Feedback' parameter to the TOP to loop.

title: Render TOP
text: Render TOP renders a 3D scene. It requires a Camera COMP, at least one Light COMP, and one or more Geometry COMPs connected to its inputs. Output is a 2D TOP. Set Camera and Light parameters to the relevant COMP paths. Add multiple geometry inputs for a scene. Anti-Alias and Render Pass parameters control quality.

title: Select TOP
text: Select TOP references another TOP without cooking it directly (a 'pass-through' reference). Set the 'TOP' parameter to the source operator path. Useful to expose a TOP at a stable path while the source changes. Python: op('select1').par.top = 'source'.

title: Transform TOP
text: Transform TOP transforms an image with Translate, Rotate, Scale, Tile and Extend modes. It is a screen-space (2D) transform, unlike Transform SOP which is geometric. Use for UV offset / rotation of textures.

title: Composite TOP
text: Composite TOP combines two or more TOPs using an Operator menu (Over, Under, Inside, Add, Subtract, Multiply, Screen, Difference, etc.). Set the Operator parameter and the number of inputs. The default Over does alpha compositing.

title: Over TOP
text: Over TOP composites one image over another with alpha. Input 0 is the foreground, input 1 the background. Faster and simpler than Composite TOP when only an 'over' is needed.

title: Mix TOP
text: Mix TOP cross-fades between two TOPs using the Cross Fade parameter (0..1). Useful for transitions between sources.

title: Threshold TOP
text: Threshold TOP converts an image to black/white (or a chosen color) based on a luminance threshold. Parameters: Threshold, Tolerance, Invert. Great after Noise/Blur for glows and particle-ish looks.

title: Ramp TOP
text: Ramp TOP generates a gradient ramp. Parameters: Type (Horizontal, Vertical, Radial, Circular), Color 1/Color 2, Softness. Use as a mask or gradient source.

title: Gradient TOP
text: Gradient TOP computes the gradient (derivative) magnitude/direction of an input image. Useful for edge detection and normal-map style effects.

title: Constant TOP
text: Constant TOP outputs a solid color. Parameters: Color, Alpha. Use as a background or color source. Python: op('constant1').par.colorr/g/b/a.

title: Circle TOP
text: Circle TOP draws a circle (or ring) with Center, Radius, Color, Antialias. Inputs can define a mask via the 'Texture' input. Useful as a shape/particle base.

title: Rectangle TOP
text: Rectangle TOP draws a rectangle with Center, Size, Color, Antialias. Use for bars, borders and UI elements.

title: Text TOP
text: Text TOP renders text to a texture. Parameters: Text, Font, Font Size, Align, Color, Background Color. Set the 'text' parameter in Python. Resolution parameter controls the output size. Use for labels, captions and HUDs.

title: Video Device In TOP
text: Video Device In TOP captures from a video input device (SDI, webcam, capture card). Parameters: Device, Signal, Resolution, Deinterlace. Requires a configured device in the Video Device In dialog. Common for live camera feeds.

title: GLSL TOP
text: GLSL TOP runs a custom GLSL shader on up to 4 input TOPs. Wire TOPs into inputs 0-3 and write a fragment shader (and optional vertex shader). Uniforms: sTD2DInputs[4], uTD2DInfos, and custom uniforms set via the 'Custom' parameter page. Output via 'fragColor'.

title: Phong MAT
text: Phong MAT is a material using the Phong lighting model (diffuse + specular). Parameters: Color, Diffuse, Specular, Roughness/Shininess, Environment Map, Maps page for textures. Apply to a Geometry COMP's Material parameter. Use with a Light COMP.

title: PBR MAT
text: PBR MAT is a physically-based material introduced for modern rendering. Parameters: Base Color, Metallic, Roughness, Emissive, Normal Map, Environment Map. More realistic than Phong MAT for lit scenes. Tagged as modern; workflows differ from Phong.

title: Constant MAT
text: Constant MAT renders geometry as a flat constant color (no lighting). Set Color and Alpha. Good for unlit overlays or mattes.

title: Depth TOP
text: Depth TOP extracts the depth buffer from a Render TOP. Set the 'Render' parameter to the Render TOP. Output is grayscale depth, useful for fog, depth-of-field or post effects.

title: Edge TOP
text: Edge TOP detects edges in an image using Sobel/Prewitt-style operators. Parameters: Method, Strength, Color. Use for outlines and stylized looks.

title: Mirror TOP
text: Mirror TOP mirrors an image horizontally/vertically. Parameters: Mirror X, Mirror Y. Use for kaleidoscope effects combined with Transform TOP.

title: Lookup TOP
text: Lookup TOP remaps an image's colors through a lookup table (LUT). Input 0 is the source, input 1 is the LUT. Use for color grading / palette mapping.

title: Displace TOP
text: Displace TOP displaces an image using a second image as the displacement map. Parameters: Displace (the map input), Horizontal/Vertical Strength. Great for water / heat-haze using a Noise TOP as the map.

title: Crop TOP
text: Crop TOP crops (or extends) an image using pixel or fraction Coordinates (Left/Right/Top/Bottom). Use to isolate regions or build grids.

title: Fit TOP
text: Fit TOP fits an image to a target resolution using a Fit parameter (Fill, Fit, Stretto/Cover, Horizontal, Vertical). Output resolution is set independently. Essential for aspect-ratio-correct layout.

title: Flip TOP
text: Flip TOP flips an image on X and/or Y. Parameters: Flip X, Flip Y. Simpler than Mirror when only a flip is needed.

title: HSV Adjust TOP
text: HSV Adjust TOP shifts Hue, Saturation and Value of an image. Parameters: Hue Adjust, Saturation Adjust, Value Adjust, with per-channel ranges. Use for color grading.

title: Cache TOP
text: Cache TOP stores the last cooked frame and can replay it. Parameters: Cache Size, Index. Useful to hold a still frame while the source updates, or step through a buffer.

title: Null TOP
text: Null TOP is a pass-through TOP used to anchor a stable output path and force a cook. It holds the last input frame. Commonly the final node before an Out TOP or a Select TOP reference.

title: Out TOP
text: Out TOP marks the final output of a TOP network. There can be multiple Out TOPs (out1..) referenced by parents. It is the boundary that exposed operators read.

title: Touch In TOP
text: Touch In TOP receives a TOP over the network from a Touch Out TOP (or another TouchDesigner instance). Parameters: Touch In (sync), Active. Use for multi-machine pipelines.

title: Touch Out TOP
text: Touch Out TOP sends a TOP over the network to a Touch In TOP. Set Active to broadcast. Pair with Touch In TOP for node-to-node streaming.

title: Audio Spectrum TOP
text: Audio Spectrum TOP visualizes an audio signal as a spectrum texture. Input is an Audio Device In CHOP / audio CHOP; output is a TOP showing frequency bins. Use for audio-reactive visuals.

title: Audio File In TOP
text: Audio File In TOP plays an audio file and exposes its waveform/spectrum as a TOP. Parameters: File, Play, Speed. Pair with Audio Spectrum TOP for reactive visuals.

title: Luma TOP
text: Luma TOP uses the luminance of one image as the alpha of another (or keys based on brightness). Parameters: Pre-multiply, Invert. Use for luminance keys.

title: Monochrome TOP
text: Monochrome TOP converts a color image to grayscale using per-channel weights. Parameters: Red/Green/Blue Weight. Use for B&W looks.

title: Chroma Key TOP
text: Chroma Key TOP removes a chosen color (green/blue screen) from an image. Parameters: Key Color, Tolerance, Softness, Spill Suppression. Standard for green-screen compositing.

title: Switch TOP
text: Switch TOP selects one of several input TOPs based on the Index parameter (0..n). Use to swap sources programmatically or via CHOP export.

title: Cross TOP
text: Cross TOP blends between two inputs with a Cross Fade parameter, like Mix TOP but optimized for two sources.

title: Sequence TOP
text: Sequence TOP plays a series of input TOPs as frames (like a flipbook) driven by an Index. Parameters: Index, Length, Sequence Mode. Use for sprite/animation playbacks.

title: Billboard TOP
text: Billboard TOP renders a TOP onto camera-facing quads in 3D, so sprites always face the camera. Used in particle/render pipelines.

title: Point Sprite TOP
text: Point Sprite TOP renders points as sprites with size attenuation, commonly fed from SOP/POP point data in render pipelines.

title: Particle TOP
text: Particle GPU TOP simulates particles entirely on the GPU. Parameters: Number, Life, Speed, Force, plus feedback of the position buffer. Output position/color TOPs are rendered via a Point Sprite / Billboard TOP. High-performance alternative to POPs for simple particle systems.

title: Video Device Out TOP
text: Video Device Out TOP sends a TOP to an output device (SDI/display). Parameters: Device, Signal, Output Resolution. Use for live show output / broadcast.

title: LFO CHOP
text: LFO CHOP generates oscillating channel values (sine, triangle, square, ramp, random). Parameters: Type, Frequency, Amplitude, Phase, Offset, Reset. Output is a channel you can export to a parameter with exportOPar. Use the 'Type' menu for waveform shape.

title: Constant CHOP
text: Constant CHOP holds a static channel value you can animate or export. Parameters: Value (per-channel), Export. Commonly exported onto TOP/COMP parameters to drive motion. Set in Python: op('constant1').par.value0 = 0.5.

title: Audio Device In CHOP
text: Audio Device In CHOP captures audio from an input device into CHOP channels (L/R etc.). Parameters: Device, Channels, Rate. Feed into Audio Spectrum CHOP or Math/Filter for audio-reactive visuals.

title: Audio Spectrum CHOP
text: Audio Spectrum CHOP computes the FFT of an audio CHOP into frequency channels. Input is an audio CHOP (e.g. Audio Device In). Parameters: FFT Size, Frequency Bins, Window. Use for audio analysis.

title: Math CHOP
text: Math CHOP performs per-channel math: Channel Pre-Op (sqrt, abs, etc.), Multiply, Add, Combine Channels, Range mapping. Use to normalize audio (range 0..1) before export or to remap values.

title: Filter CHOP
text: Filter CHOP smooths/low-pass filters channel data. Parameters: Filter (One-Pole, Box, Gaussian), Cutoff, Smooth. Use after Audio Device In to get a stable beat envelope.

title: Oscillator CHOP
text: Oscillator CHOP is a multi-waveform oscillator with phase control and is sample-rate accurate. Parameters: Type, Frequency, Amplitude, Phase. Similar to LFO but designed for audio-rate signals.

title: Noise CHOP
text: Noise CHOP generates 1D/3D noise over time per channel. Parameters: Type (Random, Turbulence, etc.), Amplitude, Frequency, Seed, Animate. Use to drive organic motion.

title: Trail CHOP
text: Trail CHOP records the recent history of a channel into a buffer of N samples, useful for oscilloscope-style displays or motion trails. Parameters: Length, Clear. Export the output to a TOP via a CHOP to TOP conversion.

title: Speed CHOP
text: Speed CHOP integrates a channel over time (or derivative with the Mode), producing a moving value from a rate. Parameters: Speed, Reset. Use to convert velocity into position.

title: Trigger CHOP
text: Trigger CHOP generates a timed envelope (attack/hold/decay) when its input crosses a threshold. Parameters: Threshold, Attack, Hold, Decay. Great for beat/event pulses.

title: Count CHOP
text: Count CHOP counts events: increments/decrements on rising edges or by step. Parameters: Count Mode, Step, Reset. Use for counters and sequencers.

title: Logic CHOP
text: Logic CHOP performs boolean/comparison logic across channels: combine with AND/OR/XOR, or convert to On/Off with a Threshold. Parameters: Channel Pre-Op, Combine Channels, Convert. Use for state machines.

title: Switch CHOP
text: Switch CHOP selects one of several input CHOPs by the Index parameter. Use to route signal sources programmatically.

title: Merge CHOP
text: Merge CHOP combines multiple CHOPs' channels. Parameters: Merge (Append, Replace, etc.), Channel Names. Use to assemble a multi-channel signal.

title: Select CHOP
text: Select CHOP extracts a subset of channels from another CHOP. Parameters: CHOP, Channel Names. Use to isolate the channels you need.

title: Analyze CHOP
text: Analyze CHOP computes statistics over channels: average, maximum, minimum, RMS, slope. Parameters: Function, Window. Use for level meters and beat detection helpers.

title: Resample CHOP
text: Resample CHOP changes the sample rate / frame mode of a CHOP. Parameters: Method, Sample Rate, Extend. Use to align audio-rate and animation-rate data.

title: Splice CHOP
text: Splice CHOP inserts/replaces a section of one CHOP with another. Parameters: Trim, Insert. Useful for editing recorded performance data.

title: Envelope CHOP
text: Envelope CHOP creates an ADSR-style envelope driven by trigger events. Parameters: Attack, Decay, Sustain, Release. Use for animation envelopes.

title: Pattern CHOP
text: Pattern CHOP generates repeating patterns (linear ramp, sine, square, etc.) across a defined length. Parameters: Type, Length, Speed. Use for stepping sequences.

title: Keyboard In CHOP
text: Keyboard In CHOP reports key states (down/up) and values from the keyboard. Parameters: Key, Channel Names. Use for interactive control.

title: Mouse In CHOP
text: Mouse In CHOP reports mouse position (u,v) and buttons. Use for cursor-driven interaction on a perform window.

title: OSC In CHOP
text: OSC In CHOP receives Open Sound Control messages over UDP. Parameters: Network Address, Port, Message Direction. Each OSC address becomes a channel. Use for phone/tablet controllers.

title: OSC Out CHOP
text: OSC Out CHOP sends CHOP channels as OSC messages over UDP. Parameters: Network Address, Port. Pair with OSC In CHOP or external OSC apps.

title: MIDI In CHOP
text: MIDI In CHOP receives MIDI notes/CC from a device. Parameters: Device, MIDI Port, Channel. Output channels represent note velocity, pitch, CC values. Use for instrument control.

title: Timer CHOP
text: Timer CHOP is a full-featured timing/sequencing engine: segments, cues, speed, go/stop. Parameters: Length, Speed, Play, Cue. The 'timer' callback drives show-control sequences. Very powerful for timelines.

title: Beat CHOP
text: Beat CHOP detects the beat/tempo from an audio input and outputs beats and BPM. Parameters: Sensitivity, Min/Max BPM. Use the 'beat' channel to trigger visuals.

title: Pulse CHOP
text: Pulse CHOP outputs a single-sample pulse (0/1) at intervals or on events. Parameters: Pulse Rate, Pulse Width. Use to trigger actions on a schedule.

title: SOP to CHOP
text: SOP to CHOP extracts SOP attributes (positions, normals, colors) into CHOP channels for further processing. Parameters: SOP, Attributes. Use for geometry-driven data.

title: CHOP to TOP
text: CHOP to TOP converts CHOP channels into a TOP image (each channel a row or column). Parameters: Format, Rows/Cols. Use to visualize data or feed shaders.

title: DAT to CHOP
text: DAT to CHOP converts table/cell data from a DAT into CHOP channels. Parameters: DAT, First Column Is, By Row/Column. Use to drive channels from spreadsheets.

title: Evaluate CHOP
text: Evaluate CHOP computes expressions per-sample across input channels. Parameters: Expressions (one per channel). Use for custom math/conditionals on signals.

title: Lookup CHOP
text: Lookup CHOP remaps an input index channel through a lookup table (another CHOP) — like a 1D sampler. Parameters: Index, Lookup CHOP. Use for wavetable/animation retiming.

title: Wave CHOP
text: Wave CHOP generates an audio-rate waveform (sine/square/saw/triangle) with frequency modulation. Used for synthesis and audio-rate modulation sources.

title: Box SOP
text: Box SOP creates a box primitive. Parameters: Size (sx,sy,sz), Center, Divisions (sx,sy,sz), Type (Polygon/Bezier/NURBS). The base primitive for most 3D scenes.

title: Sphere SOP
text: Sphere SOP creates a sphere. Parameters: Primitive Type (Polygon/Bezier/NURBS), Radius, Frequency/Rows, Orbit. Use as planets, balls, particles base.

title: Grid SOP
text: Grid SOP creates a planar grid of points/primitives. Parameters: Size, Rows, Cols, Center. Use for terrain, displacement surfaces, and point clouds.

title: Tube SOP
text: Tube SOP creates a tube (cylinder with caps or open). Parameters: Radius, Height, Rows, Caps. Use for columns, pipes and procedural shapes.

title: Torus SOP
text: Torus SOP creates a donut/torus. Parameters: Radius (major/minor), Rows, Columns. Use for rings and Orbit-style visuals.

title: Circle SOP
text: Circle SOP creates a circle/disk. Parameters: Radius, Center, Rows, Type. Use as a 2D primitive or particle disk.

title: Rectangle SOP
text: Rectangle SOP creates a rectangle in the XY plane. Parameters: Size, Center, Rows/Cols. Use for panels and flat geometry.

title: Line SOP
text: Line SOP creates a line of N points between two endpoints. Parameters: Begin/End, Points. Use for paths and connecting geometry.

title: Null SOP
text: Null SOP is a pass-through SOP used to anchor a stable output path and control cooking. Standard terminal before an Out SOP or a Geometry COMP's 'Geometry' parameter.

title: Merge SOP
text: Merge SOP combines multiple SOP streams into one. Parameters: Merge, Consolidate. Use to assemble complex geometry from primitives.

title: Switch SOP
text: Switch SOP selects one of several input SOPs by the Index parameter. Use to swap geometry programmatically.

title: Transform SOP
text: Transform SOP moves, rotates and scales geometry. Parameters: Translate, Rotate, Scale, Pivot. Input is a SOP; output is the transformed geometry. Chain multiple for compound transforms.

title: Copy SOP
text: Copy SOP replicates a source SOP many times, driven by a template (points of another SOP). Parameters: Number of Copies (or use template points), Transform. The classic instancing primitive before the instancing system.

title: Metaball SOP
text: Metaball SOP creates smooth blobby surfaces from metaball points. Parameters: Radius, Exponent, Threshold. Use for organic/liquid looks.

title: Font SOP
text: Font SOP generates 3D text geometry from a string. Parameters: Text, Font, Font Size, Extrude. Use for 3D titles.

title: Text SOP
text: Text SOP creates flat (2D) text geometry. Parameters: Text, Font, Font Size, Spacing. Use for labels in 3D space (often rendered with a Constant/Phong MAT). Note: 3D extruded text uses Font SOP.

title: Extrude SOP
text: Extrude SOP extrudes a 2D profile (e.g. from a Font/Text SOP) into 3D along a path. Parameters: Depth, Cap, Divisions. Use for 3D letters and shapes.

title: Revolve SOP
text: Revolve SOP revolves a 2D profile around an axis to create lathe-style geometry. Parameters: Axis, Start/End Angle, Divisions. Use for vases, bottles, rings.

title: Skin SOP
text: Skin SOP binds geometry to a skeleton for skeletal deformation. Parameters: Capture, Deform. Used in character/object deformation pipelines.

title: Noise SOP
text: Noise SOP displaces geometry points using 3D noise. Parameters: Transform (amp per axis), Noise Type, Frequency, Seed. Create organic deformed surfaces.

title: Point SOP
text: Point SOP edits per-point attributes (position, color, normals, scale) via expressions or parameters. Parameters: Position, Color, Scale. Core node for procedural geometry.

title: Facet SOP
text: Facet SOP controls face smoothness / consolidation and computes normals. Parameters: Cusp Polygons, Faceting, Compute Normals. Use to smooth or harden shading.

title: Join SOP
text: Join SOP connects/bridges multiple SOP surfaces into one continuous surface. Parameters: Tolerance, Consolidate. Use to weld separate primitives.

title: Divide SOP
text: Divide SOP subdivides/retessellates polygons into triangles or smaller polygons. Parameters: Triangulate, Maximum Edges. Use to clean up geometry for deformation.

title: Group SOP
text: Group SOP defines a named subset of primitives/points for later operations. Parameters: Group Name, Entity, Pattern. Use to target specific parts of geometry.

title: Delete SOP
text: Delete SOP removes primitives/points by pattern or group. Parameters: Entity, Operation, Pattern. Use to carve geometry.

title: Attribute Create SOP
text: Attribute Create SOP adds or modifies geometry attributes (color, uv, custom). Parameters: Attribute Name, Type, Value. Essential for feeding shaders and instances.

title: Peak SOP
text: Peak SOP holds the maximum value of an attribute over time (with decay). Useful to highlight peaks in motion. Parameters: Peak, Decay.

title: Carve SOP
text: Carve SOP slices a primitive at a parameter position (like extracting a cross-section). Parameters: First/Second U/V, Extract. Use for reveals and section cuts.

title: Dissolve SOP
text: Dissolve SOP gradually removes/relaxes edges based on a threshold, blending between two states. Parameters: Dissolve, Tolerance. Use for morphing/transitions.

title: Wireframe SOP
text: Wireframe SOP renders only the edges of geometry as lines. Parameters: Wire Frame, Use Corner. Use for holographic/blueprint looks.

title: Sort SOP
text: Sort SOP reorders primitives/points by criteria (position, random, attribute). Parameters: Sort By, Random Seed. Use for procedural organization and animation order.

title: Table DAT
text: Table DAT stores a 2D grid of text cells (spreadsheet). Access cells: op('table1')['r1','c1'].value or .val. Edit via .setCell() / .setRow(). Common data store for scripts.

title: Text DAT
text: Text DAT stores free-form multi-line text and can execute as a script (Python) or hold GLSL/JSON. Members: .text, .run(), .storage. Set the 'lang' parameter to 'python' to run it. The classic place to put a TouchDesigner script.

title: Script DAT
text: Script DAT runs Python each frame or on demand via the run() callback. Members: .text, .run(), .storage. Python class scriptDAT_Class. For periodic execution set the Execute parameter (On Start, On Frame Start, On Frame End, etc.).

title: Web Client DAT
text: Web Client DAT performs HTTP requests from inside TouchDesigner. Use op('web1').fetch(url, method) and handle responses in the onResponse callback. Set callbacks: onResponse, onError. Useful for talking to REST APIs, OSC bridges, or local MCP endpoints.

title: OSC In DAT
text: OSC In DAT receives OSC messages and formats them as table rows. Parameters: Network Address, Port, Address Scope. Each message becomes a row with address + arguments. Use for custom OSC parsing.

title: OSC Out DAT
text: OSC Out DAT sends OSC messages from table rows. Parameters: Network Address, Port. Pair with OSC In DAT or external OSC apps.

title: MIDI In DAT
text: MIDI In DAT receives raw MIDI events as table rows (note on/off, CC, pitch). Parameters: Device, Channel. Use for fine-grained MIDI handling beyond MIDI In CHOP.

title: UDP In DAT
text: UDP In DAT receives raw UDP packets as text rows. Parameters: Network Address, Port, Protocol. Use for custom network protocols and sensors.

title: UDP Out DAT
text: UDP Out DAT sends raw UDP packets (text) to an address. Parameters: Network Address, Port. Use for sending data to external systems.

title: TCP/IP DAT
text: TCP/IP DAT maintains a TCP client connection, sending/receiving text packets. Parameters: Address, Port, Connect. Use for robust socket communication.

title: Serial DAT
text: Serial DAT reads/writes a serial port. Parameters: Port, Baud Rate. Use for Arduino and other serial hardware.

title: File In DAT
text: File In DAT reads a text/table file from disk and updates on change. Parameters: File, Rows. Use to load CSV/JSON configuration.

title: File Out DAT
text: File Out DAT writes its contents to a file on disk. Parameters: File, Write. Use for logging and exporting data.

title: Execute DAT
text: Execute DAT runs Python on project events: On Start, On Exit, On Frame Start/End, On Movie Start/End. Use for global lifecycle hooks.

title: Panel Execute DAT
text: Panel Execute DAT runs Python on UI panel events (value change, click, rollover) of a specified panel component. Parameters: Panel, Events. Use for custom widget behavior.

title: CHOP Execute DAT
text: CHOP Execute DAT runs Python when CHOP channels cross thresholds (On Value Change, On CHOP Start/End). Parameters: CHOP, Channels. Use for event triggers.

title: Parameter Execute DAT
text: Parameter Execute DAT runs Python when parameters change (On Value Change, On Pulse). Parameters: Ops, Parameters. Use to react to UI/parameter edits.

title: Select DAT
text: Select DAT references rows/columns of another DAT without copying. Parameters: DAT, Select Rows/Columns. Use to expose a stable subset.

title: Folder DAT
text: Folder DAT lists files/folders in a directory as rows. Parameters: Folder, File Mask, Recursive. Use to build media playlists and asset browsers.

title: Multi Touch In DAT
text: Multi Touch In DAT receives tuio/multitouch events as rows. Parameters: Port. Use for touch walls and interactive surfaces.

title: Blob Track DAT
text: Blob Track DAT performs background-subtraction blob tracking from a TOP (e.g. a camera). Outputs tracked blob data as table rows. Use for interactive installations.

title: Kinect DAT
text: Kinect DAT receives skeleton/joint data from a Microsoft Kinect. Parameters: Sensor, Tracking. Use for body-tracking interactives.

title: FIFO DAT
text: FIFO DAT is a first-in-first-out queue of rows with a maximum length. Use to buffer event logs and sensor streams.

title: Oscillator DAT
text: Oscillator DAT generates a table of sine-wave sample values for audio-rate synthesis and wavetable use.

title: Container COMP
text: Container COMP is a panel/UI container that holds children and can be a 2D UI panel or a 3D object. Parameters: Background, Borders, Panel (width/height/align). The backbone of UI and nested networks.

title: Base COMP
text: Base COMP is a generic organization/extension container with no rendering or UI of its own. Use it to group logic, store extensions, and create custom components. The default choice for a custom COMP.

title: Geometry COMP
text: Geometry COMP holds SOP geometry and a material for rendering. Set the 'Material' parameter to a MAT, and 'Geometry' to a SOP. Connect it to a Render TOP via a Camera COMP and Light COMP.

title: Camera COMP
text: Camera COMP defines a viewpoint for rendering. Parameters: Position, Look At, FOV, Near/Far. Reference its path in a Render TOP's Camera parameter.

title: Light COMP
text: Light COMP defines a light source for rendering (point, distant, cone, etc.). Parameters: Light Type, Color, Intensity, Cone. Reference it in a Render TOP's Light parameter.

title: Environment Light COMP
text: Environment Light COMP lights a scene using an environment map (a TOP). Parameters: Env Map, Intensity. Use with PBR MAT for realistic reflections.

title: Null COMP
text: Null COMP is a pass-through COMP anchor (no transform change). Use it to expose a stable path and a clean input/output boundary in COMP networks.

title: Switch COMP
text: Switch COMP selects one of several input COMPs by the Index parameter. Use to swap sub-networks programmatically.

title: Select COMP
text: Select COMP references another COMP (or its output) without copying. Parameters: COMP, Select. Use to pull in geometry/UI from elsewhere at a stable path.

title: Window COMP
text: Window COMP opens a separate OS window for a render/UI network. Parameters: Window Operator (the COMP to display), Borderless, Always On Top. Used for multi-screen/performance output.

title: Perform COMP
text: Perform COMP is the root of a performance UI; its contents are shown in Perform Mode. Parameters: Children, Background. Use for kiosks and shows.

title: Panel COMP
text: Panel COMP is the base for all UI widgets; Button/Slider/Field COMPs build on it. Parameters: Panel (size, align), Value. Custom widgets subclass it.

title: Button COMP
text: Button COMP is a clickable UI button. Parameters: Button State, Momentary/Toggle, Label. Read .panel.state or .par.value0 in scripts. Use for controls.

title: Slider COMP
text: Slider COMP is a draggable value slider (horizontal/vertical). Parameters: Slider Min/Max, Value. Export its value to drive parameters.

title: Text COMP
text: Text COMP displays editable/static text in the UI. Parameters: Text, Font, Align, Editable. Use for readouts and inputs.

title: Reorder COMP
text: Reorder COMP reorders/repeats the channels or inputs passing through it. Parameters: Reorder. Use to remap data streams.

title: Keyboard In COMP
text: Keyboard In COMP captures keyboard input for a UI panel. Parameters: Key, Scope. Use within Container/Perform COMPs for interaction.

title: Mouse In COMP
text: Mouse In COMP reports mouse position within a panel. Parameters: u, v. Use for cursor-driven UI.

title: OSC In COMP
text: OSC In COMP receives OSC messages into a component's parameters/callbacks. Parameters: Port. Use for network control of a COMP.

title: Web Render COMP
text: Web Render COMP embeds a real web browser (Chromium) as a panel/texture. Parameters: URL, Reload. Use to show live web content inside TD.

title: Engine COMP
text: Engine COMP runs a separate TouchDesigner process/file as a sub-engine for parallel processing/isolation. Parameters: File, Parameters. Advanced scaling tool.

title: POP Surface
text: POP Surface is the primary POP that renders particle points/quads to the screen. Parameters: Render, Point Size, Color. It is the POP equivalent of a Render TOP for particles.

title: POP Location
text: POP Location generates particle positions (a point cloud) as the starting state of a particle system. Parameters: Generate (Grid/Sphere/Random), Count. Feed into a POP Solver.

title: POP Solver
text: POP Solver simulates particles over time: integrates velocity, applies forces, handles life/collision. Parameters: Life, Forces, Collide. The simulation core of a POP network.

title: POP Force
text: POP Force applies forces (gravity, curl noise, attraction) to particles in a POP network. Parameters: Force, Noise, Turbulence. Connect into the POP Solver's force input.

title: POP Trigger
text: POP Trigger emits/scatters particles on events (e.g. when a CHOP crosses a threshold). Parameters: Emit Rate, On Event. Use for reactive bursts.

title: POP Stream Out
text: POP Stream Out exports the current particle state (positions/attributes) to a SOP or CHOP for rendering/analysis. Parameters: Output. Bridges POPs back to classic pipelines.

title: POP Convert
text: POP Convert converts SOP geometry into POP points (and vice versa), letting you mix particle and geometry workflows.

title: POPs family overview
text: POPs are the Particle Operators family, introduced in the 2023.10000 build. They generate and simulate particles (POP Surface, POP Location, POP Solver, POP Force). Parameter identifiers and workflows differ from SOPs; do not assume SOP naming applies. Use a POP Render TOP or convert to SOPs for rendering.

title: OP_Class
text: OP_Class is the base class for all operators in the TouchDesigner Python API. Key members: .par (parameters), .name, .path, .parent(), .children, .create(name, type) to make a child operator, .copy(ops) to duplicate, .destroy() to delete, .change() to force cook. tops/chops/sops/dats/comps properties give typed children lists. Use op('name') or parent().op('name') to fetch an operator.

title: COMP_Class
text: COMP_Class is the Python class for Container/COMP operators. Members inherit OP_Class and add: .create(), .children (COMP children), .extensions, .panel (panel members for UI), .par for COMP parameters. Access via op('container1').

title: TOP_Class
text: TOP_Class is the Python class for TOP operators. Members: .width, .height (cooked resolution), .aspect, .gpuMemory, .par for parameters. Access via op('top1'). Some TOPs (moviefileinTOP_Class) add clip-specific members.

title: CHOP_Class
text: CHOP_Class is the Python class for CHOP operators. Members: .numChannels, .numSamples, .sampleRate, .channel[index] / .chan(name), .vals, .par. Access channels with op('chop1')['channame'].

title: SOP_Class
text: SOP_Class is the Python class for SOP operators. Members: .numPoints, .numPrims, .points, .prims, .par. Geometry iteration uses .points and .prims with .P (position), .N (normal), .Cd (color) attributes.

title: DAT_Class
text: DAT_Class is the Python class for DAT operators. Members: .text, .rows, .cols, .cell(row,col), .setCell(), .appendRow(), .clear(). Access cell value: op('table1')[0,'col'].value. Use .text for plain text.

title: Par_Class
text: Par_Class represents a single parameter object. Members: .val/.value (current value), .name, .expr (expression string), .mode (constant/expression/binding/export), .enable, .pulse(). Set value: op('top1').par.rotatez = 0.5. Set expression: op('x').par.scale.expr = 'me.time.frame*0.01'.

title: Cell_Class
text: Cell_Class represents one cell of a Table DAT. Members: .val (string), .value (typed: number if numeric), .row, .col, .owner. Set: op('t')[0,0].val = 'hello'.

title: Channel_Class
text: Channel_Class represents one CHOP channel. Members: .val/.value (current sample), .name, .samples (all samples), .evalFrame(f), .min/.max. Access: op('chop1')['chan'].value.

title: TDStoreTools / storage
text: storage is a per-operator dictionary for persisting data across cooks and frames: op('x').store('key', value) and op('x').fetch('key'). It survives cook passes and is the recommended way to cache state (vs globals). Also see the `extension` mechanism on Base/COMP for object-oriented state.

title: ui module
text: The `ui` module controls the TouchDesigner UI/runtime. Members: ui.message(text), ui.status, ui.panes, ui.viewers, ui.undo (the undo context manager), ui.performMode. Example: with ui.undo: op('x').par.value0 = 1 — wraps mutations in a single Ctrl+Z-able undo block.

title: project module
text: The `project` module is the global project object. Members: project.cook (the timeline/cook state), project.name, project.folder, project.save(), project.load(path). project.cook.play toggles playback; project.cook.rate is fps; project.cook.frame is the current frame.

title: me object
text: The `me` object refers to the operator whose script is executing (the 'current' OP). Members: me.time (the time of the current cook), me.parent(), me.var. In a Text DAT script, `me` is that DAT. In parameter expressions, `me` is the operator owning the parameter.

title: op() and find() functions
text: Global lookup functions: op('path') returns an operator by absolute/relative path; op.WToggle not needed. parent() returns the parent COMP; parent(2) the grandparent. root returns the root. findChildren() / findCell() search by name pattern and tags. Use op.TDResources for shared modules. For remote/path lookups use op('opname') and op('/project1/x').

title: App_Class
text: App_Class is the application-level object accessible as `app`. Members: app.samples (sample rate), app.help, app.config, app.updateAll(). Represents the running TouchDesigner application instance.

title: timeClass (me.time)
text: timeClass represents a cook's time context, accessed as me.time. Members: .frame (current frame), .seconds, .rate (fps), .play. Use in expressions/scripts to drive animation: me.time.frame.

title: dependencies / Par bindings
text: Parameters can be linked with Bindings (TD 2022+): set a parameter's mode to 'Bind' and bind it to op('other').par.value. Dependencies (td.Par / tdu.Dependency) propagate updates reactively. Custom parameters are added via the 'Custom' parameter page of an operator (Edit Parameter Dialog).

title: tdu module
text: The `tdu` module holds utilities: tdu.Dependency(val), tdu.FileTypes, tdu.splitString, tdu.radians()/tdu.degrees(), tdu.clamp(x,lo,hi), tdu.remap(). Useful helpers for Python scripting.

title: scriptDAT_Class
text: scriptDAT_Class is the Python class for a Script DAT. Members: .text (script source), .run() (execute), .storage, .module (the executed module). Set Execute parameter for when it runs (On Start, On Frame Start, etc.).

title: moviefileinTOP_Class
text: moviefileinTOP_Class is the Python class for a Movie File In TOP. Members: .par.file, .par.play, .par.speed, .width, .height (cooked resolution), .numFrames, .cueFrame. Access via op('moviein1'). Read .width/.height to get actual loaded video pixel size.

title: Keyboard / Mouse panels (panel members)
text: UI panel components expose a `panel` member: op('button1').panel.state (0/1), .u/.v (cursor in panel), .inside, .focus. Use these in Panel Execute DATs and Python to build responsive UI.

title: extension classes
text: Custom COMP behavior is added via Extensions: a Text DAT with a Python class is attached in the COMP's 'Extension' parameter. Access via op('comp').MyExt or op('comp').extensions. Extensions hold persistent methods/state for reusable components.

title: GLSL fragment template
text: GLSL TOP fragment shader template: uniform sampler2D sTD2DInputs[4]; out vec4 fragColor; void main() { vec2 uv = vUV.st; vec4 c = texture(sTD2DInputs[0], uv); fragColor = vec4(c.rgb * 1.2, c.a); }. Use uTD2DInfos[i].res for resolution. Wire a TOP into input 0. Output via 'fragColor'.

title: GLSL vertex shader
text: GLSL TOP vertex shader: use 'in vec3 P;' and 'out vec2 vUV;' to pass UVs to the fragment shader. The default pipeline provides P (position) and UV attributes. Modify gl_Position for vertex displacement. Declare 'uniform float uTime;' and set it from the GLSL TOP's Custom parameter page.

title: GLSL multiple inputs
text: A GLSL TOP can read up to 4 input TOPs: sTD2DInputs[0..3]. Sample each with texture(sTD2DInputs[i], uv). Blend them in the fragment shader. uTD2DInfos[i] holds per-input {res, clamp, depth}. Use for multi-texture effects.

title: GLSL custom uniforms
text: Custom uniforms in a GLSL TOP are declared in the shader (uniform float uAmount;) and bound via the GLSL TOP's 'Custom' parameter page: add 'uAmount' and set its value or export a CHOP channel to it. They update live without recompiling.

title: GLSL PBR / lighting
text: For lit custom shaders use the PBR/Phong MAT's 'Lighting' via GLSL MAT, or compute lighting manually in a GLSL TOP. Phong-style: diffuse = max(dot(N, L), 0.0). A GLSL MAT lets a Geometry COMP use a fully custom shader with scene lighting and shadows.

title: GLSL MAT
text: GLSL MAT is a material that uses a custom vertex+fragment shader for rendering geometry (like a GLSL TOP but in the 3D pipeline). Wire into a Geometry COMP's Material parameter. Provides world/view/projection matrices and attribute varyings.

title: CHOP Export to Parameters
text: Export CHOP channels onto operator parameters so data drives visuals. Right-click a CHOP channel and choose Export, or in Python: op('chan1').exportOPar('null1', 'tx'). Exported parameters show a blue 'x' overlay. Clear with op('null1').unExport('tx'). Great for audio-reactive motion.

title: Connecting operators in Python
text: Wire operators in Python: set op('null1').inputs[0] = op('noise1'), or use op('null1').inputConnectors[0].connect(op('noise1')). For outputs, op('null1').outputs[0].connect(op('out1')). Check valid connections with .validConnectors. Duplicate with .copy() before wiring to avoid breaking existing graphs.

title: Audio-reactive recipe
text: Audio-reactive setup: Audio Device In CHOP -> Math CHOP (normalize) -> Filter CHOP. Export the filtered channel onto a TOP or COMP parameter with exportOPar, e.g. op('audi1').exportOPar('circle1', 'radius'). Use a Level TOP driven by the beat for a pulse effect.

title: Feedback trails recipe
text: Feedback trails: Noise TOP -> Level TOP (decay) -> Feedback TOP input0; Feedback TOP input1 gets the Level TOP output (the loop). Feedback TOP output -> another Level/Threshold for glow -> Out. Raise Level's Post slightly each frame to keep a fading trail.

title: Render pipeline recipe
text: Basic 3D render: Geometry COMP (with Material = Phong MAT, Geometry = a SOP) + Camera COMP + Light COMP -> Render TOP. Add a Null TOP after Render TOP as the stable output. Set the Render TOP's Camera and Light parameters to the COMP paths.

title: Instancing recipe
text: GPU instancing: create a single SOP (e.g. Box SOP) in a Geo COMP, set the Geo's 'Instance' parameter to a CHOP or DAT providing per-instance transforms (tx,ty,tz,rx,ry,rz,sx,sy,sz). Thousands of copies render in one draw call. Drive the instance data from a CHOP to Table DAT or a SOP to CHOP.

title: OSC receive recipe
text: Receive OSC: OSC In CHOP (set Port, e.g. 8000) -> channels appear per OSC address. Or OSC In DAT for raw messages. Map channels onto parameters via Export. Use a phone app (TouchOSC) sending to the machine's IP and that port.

title: Web API request recipe
text: Call a REST API: Web Client DAT. Set op('web1').par.url and call op('web1').fetch(url, method='GET'). Handle the result in the onResponse callback, parse JSON with the stdlib json module, and write to a Table DAT. Use for live data feeds.

title: Particle render recipe (POPs)
text: POPs particle system: POP Location (generate points) -> POP Force (gravity/noise) -> POP Solver (simulate) -> POP Surface (render). Add a Light COMP + Camera COMP + Render TOP (set to the POP Surface) to view. Tagged version 2023.10000+.

title: Particle render recipe (GPU TOP)
text: GPU particle system without POPs: Particle GPU TOP (Number, Life, Force) -> Point Sprite TOP / Billboard TOP -> Render TOP with a Camera. This runs entirely on the GPU and works on older builds.

title: Projection mapping basics
text: Projection mapping: render content with a Render TOP using a Camera COMP placed at the projector; feed the Render TOP into a Projector COMP or directly to a Video Device Out TOP. Calibrate the camera's FO/position to the physical projector. Use Null TOPs as stable outputs.

title: Data-driven UI recipe
text: Build a data-driven UI: load a CSV with File In DAT / parse JSON from Web Client DAT into a Table DAT; use it to populate Button/Slider COMPs in a Container COMP. Read cell values with op('table1')[r,c].value in callbacks.

title: Undo-safe mutation pattern
text: Wrap any Python mutation in an undo block so one Ctrl+Z reverts the whole batch: 'from td import *' not needed; use 'with ui.undo: op("x").par.value0 = 1; op("y").par.tx = 2'. This matches the safe-by-default bridge design and lets agents recover from mistakes.

title: Version-aware docs note
text: TouchDesigner features are build-gated: POPs require >= 2023.10000; PBR MAT / Environment Light / Bindings >= 2022.20000; Web Render COMP >= 2021.10000. Always check min_version when applying a recipe to an older project to avoid referencing operators that don't exist there.

title: Timeline control
text: The TouchDesigner timeline is accessed via me.time or project.cook. Set playback with project.cook.play = True/False, project.cook.rate for fps, me.time.frame for current frame. Python class for time is timeClass. Range controls loop length. Use Timer CHOP or Run TOP for sequence control.

title: Custom parameter creation
text: Add custom parameters to any operator via the parameter dialog (click the + on the 'Custom' page) or in Python via the Edit Parameter Dialog. Custom params appear on .par like built-ins and can be exported/bound. Use them to expose component controls cleanly.

title: CHOP to TOP visualization
text: Visualize data: CHOP to TOP converts channels into an image (each channel a row). Pipe Audio Spectrum CHOP -> CHOP to TOP for a live spectrum display, or Trail CHOP -> CHOP to TOP for an oscilloscope. Then place the TOP on screen via a Container/Perform COMP.

title: Multi-instance / remote control
text: Control a remote TouchDesigner: run a Touch Out TOP/CHOP to stream data, and a Touch In TOP/CHOP on the receiver. For command control, use a Web Server DAT or OSC In CHOP receiving from another instance. Single base URL + port routing handles multiple instances (see bridge design).

title: Perform Mode / Windowing
text: For shows: build the UI under a Perform COMP, then enter Perform Mode (F1/ctrl+F1) to display just that network fullscreen. Use a Window COMP for secondary displays and Video Device Out TOP for broadcast outputs. Set the Perform COMP as the perform target in Project Settings.

title: Ableton Link CHOP
text:
Ableton Link CHOP (CHOP). The Ableton Link CHOP retrieves timing information from an Ableton Link supported network.		
For more information see:  http://www.ableton.com/en/link/ Parameters (27): Active, Enable, Start Stop Sync Enable, Signature, Callbacks DAT, Status Channels, Ramp, Pulse, Sine, Count, Count+Ramp, Bar, Beat, Sixteenths, Ramp Bar, Ramp Beat, Tempo, Beats, Phase, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method.

title: Accumulate POP
text: Accumulate POP (POP). The Accumulate POP takes an attribute from the input, and creates a new attribute whose values in each point are the sum of the values in the input attribute of the previous points. For example, if an input has 100 points, and an attribute called Steps contains the same number, say 3 for each point, then the output attribute Scan will contain 3, 6, and 9 for the first three points, and 300 for the last point. It is the running sum or "integral". If you change Type to Exclusive Scan, it will not count the current point's value, so the Scan attribute will contain 0, 3, 6 up to 297 in the last point. The Accumulate POP can work on a point, vertex or primitive attribute. If the input is a float3, the new attribute will be float3, etc. You can check it all with a [[POP to DAT]]. See also: [[Analyze POP]], [[Histogram POP]] Parameters (25): Steps, attrclass, point, inputattr, scantype, inclusive, outputattrscope, P, overrideautoattr, newattrtype, float, attrnumcomps, 1, newattrdefaultval, newattrdefaultval1, bypass, delinputattrs, parmcolorspace, srgb, parmreferencewhite, default, Attribute Class, Input Attribute Scope, Type.

title: Actor COMP
text: Actor COMP (COMP). An Actor COMP is analogous to a body (or bodies) in a physics system. An Actor COMP must be used in conjunction with a physics solver: either a Bullet Solver COMP or Nvidia Flex Solver COMP, which in turn is analogous to the world/simulation that the actors/bodies operate in. An Actor COMP can either be static, meaning it is not affected by any forces in the simulation and cannot move (ie. has infinite mass), or it can be dynamic, meaning it is moved by forces and collides with other bodies (either static or dynamic) in the world. Parameters (158): Initialize Actor, Update Collision Shape, Active, Kinematic State, Static (Infinite Mass), Collision SOPs, Collision Shape, Concave (Static only), Ellipsoid Tolerance, Infinite Mass, Mass, Cue Velocity, Cue Pulse, Linear Velocity, Angular Velocity, Forces, Use Global Gravity, Gravitational Acceleration, Friction, Rolling Friction, Restitution, Continuous Collision Detection, Display Guide, Center of Mass.

title: Add SOP
text: Add SOP (SOP). The Add SOP can both create new Points and Polygons on its own, or it can be used to add Points and Polygons to an existing input. Parameters (19): Points DAT, Named Attributes, Delete Geometry, Keep Points, Add Points, Point, Position, Weight, Method, By Group, Group, Add, All Points, N, Closed, Polygons Table, Polygon, Pattern, Remove Unused Points, Compute Normals.

title: Add TOP
text: Add TOP (TOP). The Add TOP composites the input images together by adding the pixel values. Output = Input1 + Input2. It clamps a color channel if the sum exceeds 1. Parameters (34): Fixed Layer, Input 1, Pre-Fit Overlay, Fill, Justify Horizontal, Left, Justify Vertical, Bottom, Extend Overlay, Hold, Rotate, Translate, X, Translate Units, Scale, Pivot, Pivot Units, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect.

title: Alembic SOP
text: Alembic SOP (SOP). The Alembic SOP loads and plays back Alembic file geometry sequences. Parameters (10): Alembic File, Object Path, Time, Time Unit, Transform, None, Interpolation, Straight to GPU, Compute Normal, Unload.

title: Align SOP
text: Align SOP (SOP). The Align SOP aligns a group of primitives to each other or to an auxiliary input, by translating or rotating each primitive along any pivot point. Parameters (19): Group, Align, All Primitives, N, Bias, Left UV, U, Right UV, Right UV End, Individual Alignment, Translate, Rotate, Transform Order, Scale Rotate Translate, Rotate Order, Rx Ry Rz, X, Scale, Pivot.

title: Ambient Light COMP
text: Ambient Light COMP (COMP). The Ambient Light Component controls the color and intensity of the environmental light in a given scene. This light, unlike the Light Component, has no particular source. The light it sheds comes from everywhere as opposed to a point light source or focused spotlight. Parameters (35): Light Color, Red, Dimmer, Material, Render, Draw Priority, Pick Priority, Wireframe Color, Light Mask, Extension, Object, Name, Promote, Re-Init Extensions, Parent Shortcut, Global OP Shortcut, Internal OP, Shortcut, OP, Node View, Default Viewer, Operator Viewer, Enable Cloning, Enable Cloning Pulse.

title: Analyze CHOP
text: Analyze CHOP (CHOP). The Analyze CHOP looks at the values of all the values of a channel, and outputs a single-number result into the output. The output is one sample long. It can analyze for maximum, average, peaks and other aspects of a channel. Parameters (14): Function, Average, Allow Start Peaks, Allow End Peaks, No Peak Value, Analyze Valleys vs Peaks, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Analyze POP
text: Analyze POP (POP). The Analyze POP analyzes any point, vertex or primitive attributes of a POP, and outputs a single point containing the resulting statistics. It typically computes an average, minimum or maximum values of any attribute. Attach an Analyze POP to a [[POP to DAT]] (and set its Transpose) to see what it comes up with. If you select more than one attribute, or if you select all (*), it will append the attribute names of the input POP to all the output attribute names. Tip: The results on the Analyze POP are a single point on the GPU. What you do with it next affects performance. The more you stay on the GPU the faster it will be. Pulling the results to the CPU and using them in parameters will be slower. You can get it to the CPU on the next frame (faster) by using a POP to CHOP but of course it's delayed. Or you can fully stay in the GPU’s POP world by passing the point as an input to other operators like to a Math Mix POP. In the case of an attribute with multiple components, the analysis is per component. For example for P, Min(0) is the min X value, Min(1) is the min Y value. indexMin(0) is the index of the point with the min X value, IndexMin(1) is the index of the point with the min Y value. It can optionaly output the sum and power of the samples of the input attribute. As noted above it can output the index of the point (the point number) of the sample with the maximum or minimum value of the selected input attribute. It is also possible to combine the components of a vector to a single component before performing the analysis, using the Combine Components menu. '''Note''': Palette: popViewer can show the ranges of all attributes. See also: [[Accumulate POP]], [[Histogram POP]] Parameters (49): P, attrsclass, point, group, numgroupelements, inputattrs, *, appendattrname, combine, off, avg, centroid, min, max, size, minindex, maxindex, sum, rmspower, numpointsvertsprims, numprimsbatch, dimension, pattrvals, none.

title: Analyze TOP
text: Analyze TOP (TOP). The Analyze TOP takes any image and determines various characteristics of it, such as the average pixel color, the pixel with the maximum luminance, or the min and max values in each channel. The result is stored in a 1x1, 1xN or Nx1 image depending on the chosen scope (See Scope parameter below). The calculated values can be brought into CHOPs using the TOP to CHOP node. Parameters (26): Operation, Average, Analyze Channel, Luminance, Scope, Full Image, Exclude NaNs, Mask, None, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes.

title: Angle CHOP
text: Angle CHOP (CHOP). The Angle CHOP is a general purpose converter between degrees, radians, quaternions and vectors. Different formats assume a specific ordering of input channels. Parameters (14): Input Units, Degrees, Input Order, Rx Ry Rz, Output Units, Output Order, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Animation COMP
text: Animation COMP (COMP). The Animation Component is a special component used for creating keyframe animation channels. The component contains a pre-defined network utilizing a Keyframe CHOP and a number of Table DATs to define the animated CHOP channels. Parameters (51): Time Reference, Play Mode, Locked to Timeline, Play, Speed, Cue, Cue Pulse, Cue Point, Cue Point Unit, Input Index Unit, Samples, Cyclic Range, Automatic, Specify Edit Attributes, Edit Origin, Edit Rate, Edit Animation..., Type, Timeline, Start, Start Unit, End, End Unit, Trim Left.

title: Annotate COMP
text: Annotate COMP (COMP). Annotate COMP operator for TouchDesigner Parameters (61): Title Text, Title Height, Title Align, Left, Body Text, Body Font Size, Limit Body Text Width, Max Body Text Width, Mode, Comment, Smart Quote, Body Word Wrap, Back Color, Back Color Alpha, Annotate Opacity, Viewer Display, OP, OP Viewer Interactive, Size/Aspect Override, Natural, Size/Aspect, Scale, Justify X, Justify Y.

title: Anti Alias TOP
text: Anti Alias TOP (TOP). The Anti-Alias TOP uses a screen space antialiasing technique called ‘SMAA: Enhanced Subpixel Morphological Antialiasing’. Detailed information here: http://www.iryoku.com/smaa/  It uses an image’s luminance, color or a depth map to detect edges of objects and then applies anti-aliasing to those edges. The current implementation does not yet use the temporal features of it, so each image is individually anti-aliased without requiring knowledge of other frames. It doesn’t require the extra memory that regular Render TOP anti-aliasing does, and avoids work involved with antialiasing polygon edges that are ultimately occluded by another object. Parameters (26): Quality, Low, Edge Detect Source, Luminance, Edge Threshold, Max Search Steps, Max Diag Search Steps, Corner Rounding, Output Edges, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes.

title: Arm SOP
text: Arm SOP (SOP). The Arm SOP creates all the necessary geometry for an arm, and provides a smooth, untwisted skin that connects the arm to the body. It is controlled through inverse kinematics linked to a handprint. Parameters (36): Capture Type, Ellipses, Arm Axis, + X, Radius, Rotate Hand, Auto Elbow Twist, Elbow Twist, Flip Elbow, Clavicle, Humerous, Ulna, Hand, Shoulder Joint, Elbow Joint, Wrist Joint, Shoulder 1, X, Shoulder 2, Shoulder 3, Shoulder 4, Shoulder 5, Elbow 1, Elbow 2.

title: Art-Net DAT
text: Art-Net DAT (DAT). The Art-Net DAT polls and lists all devices on the network. Parameters (9): Callbacks DAT, Columns, Poll Devices, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: Attribute CHOP
text: Attribute CHOP (CHOP). The Attribute CHOP adds, removes or updates attributes of the input CHOP. Currently there is only one attribute type, a "quaternion". This attribute type is used to group rotation channel triplets (rx,ry,rz) together. Parameters (12): Function, Pass Through, Rotate Order, Rx Ry Rz, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Attribute Combine POP
text: Attribute Combine POP (POP). The Attribute Combine POP takes multiple POP inputs and lets you choose attributes from each input by name or by pattern matching, and send the attributes to the output. On the Inputs page is a set of sequential blocks to specify the inputs. For each block, you can either wire one input to the node, or using its In POP(s) parameter you can specify one or more POPs using pattern matching. For each block you can use the In Attributes and pattern matching to specify which attribute(s) you want from that input. You can also specify a new name to rename the attributes. For each block, if there is a '''name conflict''' with a prior input, the Duplicate Attributes menu will give you the choice to make up a new incremented attribute name, to use the first or last occurance of an attribute, or use only the first input's attribute. '''Point, Vertex or Primitive Attributes''': All attributes of the first in put are sent to then output. The from the Attribute Class menu you choose which attribute type you want to pass to the output (one only) from the rest of the outputs. For example you an merge the primitive attribute from all the inputs. The '''Length Mismatch''' parameter lets you choose what to do when, say the input POPs don't have the same number of points. You can choose to not infor the user (None), or put a Warning on the node, or put an Error on the node. In any case, when there is a mismatch, for shorter inputs, you can choose to hold the values of the last points of the attributes, make them 0, make them 1, or do nothing. This '''architecture for handling multiple inputs''' to POPs in the Attribute Combine POP is also part of numerous other POPs, such as the [[Math Combine POP]], [[Math Mix POP]], [[Blend POP]], [[Merge POP]] and [[Switch POP]]. Parameters (23): attrclass, point, lengthmismatchnotif, ignore, lengthmismatchaction, hold, duplicateattrs, autorename, input, input0pop, input0attrs, *, input0renameto, bypass, delinputattrs, parmcolorspace, srgb, parmreferencewhite, default, Attribute Class, Length Mismatch, Duplicate Attributes, In.

title: Attribute Convert POP
text: Attribute Convert POP (POP). The Attribute Convert POP lets you copy a POP [[attribute]] from one "attribute class" to another. That is, attributes can be on a point, a vertex, or a primitive. You can, for example convert a primitive attribute to the points of that primitive. In some cases it is ambiguous, like when a point is shared between multiple primitives or multiple vertices, so the POP picks one value and doesn't complain. When it converts, it can delete the original attribute, and it can error or warning if the attribute it creates already exists. See the [[POP to DAT]] to find all the attributes of a POP. See [[Attribute POP]] to copy or rename an attribute within one attribute class. Note: There currently are no attributes on the POP as a whole. Parameters (21): convertop, none, attrname, *, newattrname, deleteorig, notificationifexists, ignore, overrideifexists, bypass, delinputattrs, parmcolorspace, srgb, parmreferencewhite, default, Convert Operation, Input Attributes, New Attribute Name, Delete Original, Notification if Attribute Exists, Override if Attribute Exists.

title: Attribute Create SOP
text: Attribute Create SOP (SOP). The Attribute Create SOP allows you to add normals or tangents to geometry. Parameters (3): Compute Normals, Compute Tangents, Use MikkTSpace.

title: Attribute POP
text: Attribute POP (POP). The Attribute POP lets you: * Create new attributes * Rename attributes * Duplicate attributes * Delete attributes * Create matrix attributes Refer to [[Attribute]]. When you create a new attribute, you specify: * the name (choose from built-in pre-defined names, or create your own) * the type (float, double, int, uint) * the number of components in the attribute (1, 2, 3 or 4 only). Examples: ** A float type with 3 components is therefore a float3. ** A double with 2 components is a double2. ** An int with 4 components is an ivec4. ** A uint with 3 components is a uint3. * arrays of the above, for example create a array of 8 float3, appearing as float3[8]. The array length can be anything. * the default value for each component of the attribute New attributes can also be matrices (done on the Matrix page), like creating a mat4x4. Parameters (55): attrclass, point, group, notificationifexists, ignore, overrideifexists, premultcolor, attr, attr0name, custom, attr0customname, attr0type, float, attr0numcomps, 1, attr0isarray, attr0arraysize, attr0value, attr0valuer, mat, mat0name, mat0numrows, mat0numcols, mat0isarray.

title: Attribute SOP
text: Attribute SOP (SOP). The Attribute SOP allows you to manually rename and delete point, vertex, and primitive attributes. Parameters (8): Delete Attributes, *, Point Rename, From Attribute, To Attribute, Vertex Rename, Prim Rename, Detail Rename.

title: Audio Band EQ CHOP
text: Audio Band EQ CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Band EQ CHOP is a 16-band equalizer which filters audio input channels in the same way that a conventional band (graphic) equalizer uses a bank of sliders to filter fixed-frequency bands of sound. Parameters (25): Dry / Wet Mix, 25 Hz, 40 Hz, 60 Hz, 90 Hz, 150 Hz, 240 Hz, 370 Hz, 590 Hz, 930 Hz, 1.5 KHz, 2.3 KHz, 3.6 KHz, 5.6 KHz, 8.9 KHz, 14 KHz, 22 KHz, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root.

title: Audio Binaural CHOP
text: Audio Binaural CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Binaural CHOP uses the Steam Audio API to convert from multi-channel speaker-based audio (eg. stereo, quadraphonic, 5.1, 7.1, etc.) to binaural using HRTF-based binaural rendering. The HRTF used is the default provided by Steam Audio. Parameters (14): Active, Input Format, Stereo, Ambisonics Order, Listener, Mapping Table, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio Device In CHOP
text: Audio Device In CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Device In CHOP receives audio from any of the attached audio input devices using DirectSound/CoreAudio or ASIO. It always outputs time sliced audio data. Parameters (37): Active, Driver, DirectSound/CoreAudio, Device, Error if Missing, Inputs, Format, Mono, Rate, Buffer Length, Number of Channels, Front Left, Front Right, Front Center, Low Frequency, Back Left, Back Right, Front Left of Center, Front Right of Center, Back Center, Side Left, Side Right, Top Center, Top Front Left.

title: Audio Device Out CHOP
text:
Audio Device Out CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Device Out CHOP sends audio to any of the attached audio output devices using DirectSound/CoreAudio or ASIO. 			
The second input on the Audio Device Out CHOP can be used for volume control. Parameters (38): Active, Driver, DirectSound, Device, Error if Missing, Outputs, Buffer Length, Volume, Pan, Clamp Output, Cook Every Frame, Stereo Mode, Front Left, Front Right, Front Center, Low Frequency, Back Left, Back Right, Front Left of Center, Front Right of Center, Back Center, Side Left, Side Right, Top Center.

title: Audio Devices DAT
text: Audio Devices DAT (DAT). The Audio Devices DAT provides information about all detected audio devices including driver, direction (input or output), label, and name that can be referenced in the Audio Device In CHOP and Audio Device Out CHOP. Parameters (12): Driver, default (DirectSound/CoreAudio), All Drivers, Input Devices, Output Devices, Callbacks DAT, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: Audio Dynamics CHOP
text:
Audio Dynamics CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Dynamics CHOP is designed to control the dynamic range of an audio signal. Dynamic range refers to how loud and quiet the audio is over some period of time. The Operator contains two types of dynamic control: compression and limiting.		
It is recommended that you link this CHOP to an Info CHOP, so that you can have some visual feedback:  The amount of compression or limiting which is being applied will be displayed in the Info CHOP. Parameters (23): Input Gain (dB), Enable Compressor, Compression Type, Automatic Gain Control, Channel Linking, Compress Equally, Threshold (dB), Ratio, Knee, Attack (msec=10**val), Release (msec=10**val), Output Gain (dB), Enable Limiter, Limit Equally, Dry / Wet Mix, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio File In CHOP
text: Audio File In CHOP (CHOP). The Audio File In CHOP reads audio from files on disk or at http:// addresses. File types .mp3, .aif, .aiff, .au, and .wav files are supported. It always outputs time sliced audio data. If you want to record the data, use a Record CHOP or Movie File Out TOP. Parameters (33): File, Reload, Play, Play Mode, Locked to Timeline, Speed, Cue, Cue Pulse, Cue Point, Cue Point Unit, Index, Index Unit, Timecode Object/CHOP/DAT, Repeat, Off, Trim, Trim Start, Trim Start Unit, Trim End, Trim End Unit, Pre-Read Length, Pre-Read Length Unit, Open Timeout, Mono.

title: Audio File Out CHOP
text: Audio File Out CHOP (CHOP). The Audio File Out CHOP saves an audio stream out to a file using a variety of different codecs. Parameters (19): File Type, WAV, Unique Suffix, N, File, Codec, Uncompressed 16-bit (PCM), Bit Rate, 96 kb/s, Record, Pause, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio Filter CHOP
text: Audio Filter CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Filter CHOP removes low frequencies, high frequencies, both low and high, or removes a mid-frequency range. Parameters (17): Filter, Low Pass, Cutoff Units, Logarithmic, Filter Cutoff (Hz=10**val), Filter Cutoff (Hz), Filter Resonance, Roll-Off (dB per Octave), Dry / Wet Mix, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio Movie CHOP
text: Audio Movie CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Movie CHOP plays the audio of a movie file that is played back with a Movie File In TOP. Use the Movie File In TOP parameter to specify which Movie File In TOP to get the audio signal from. Parameters (17): Play, Movie File In TOP, Pre-Read Length, Pre-Read Length Unit, Open Timeout, Audio Sync Offset, Audio Sync Offset Unit, Index Channel, Audio Track Index, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio NDI CHOP
text: Audio NDI CHOP (CHOP). Retrieves the audio from a NDI In TOP's NDI stream. The audio signal can then be modified or output via CHOPs.  See NDI. Parameters (10): Play, NDI In TOP, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio Oscillator CHOP
text: Audio Oscillator CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Oscillator CHOP generates sounds in three ways. It repeats common waveforms (sine, triangle), it generates white noise (a random number for each sample), or it repeats a prepared incoming audio clip of any duration. It outputs typically 44100 samples per second. In contrast, the LFO CHOP by default generates waves at lower frequencies and lower sample rates (60 samples/second), however both Audio Oscillator and LFO are interchangeable by changing their frequencies and sample rates. Parameters (22): Type, Sine, Base Frequency, Units per Octave, Offset, Amplitude, Bias, Phase, Smooth Pitch Changes, Reset Condition, Off to On, Reset, Reset Pulse, Sample Rate, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio Para EQ CHOP
text: Audio Para EQ CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Para EQ CHOP (parametric equalizer) applies up to 3 parametric filters to the incoming sound. The three filters are in series, where internally, the second filter takes its input from the output of the first filter, and so on. Parameters (26): Frequency Units, Logarithmic, Enable EQ1, Boost (dB) EQ1, Frequency (Hz=10**val) EQ1, Frequency (Hz) EQ1, Bandwidth EQ1, Enable EQ2, Boost (dB) EQ2, Frequency (Hz=10**val) EQ2, Frequency (Hz) EQ2, Bandwidth EQ2, Enable EQ3, Boost (dB) EQ3, Frequency (Hz=10**val) EQ3, Frequency (Hz) EQ3, Bandwidth EQ3, Dry / Wet Mix, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index.

title: Audio Play CHOP
text: Audio Play CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Play CHOP plays back a sound file through any attached audio output device using DirectSound. It supports .aif, .mp3, .mid, .wav and .m4a files up to 48.000 kHz, which can have mono, stereo or even up to 5.1 channels in them. The audio channels can then be routed to any speaker location that DirectSound uses. See Outputs section below. Parameters (36): Device, Outputs, Sound File, DAT List, Volume, Mode, Play While On, Trigger, Cook Every Frame, Stereo Mode, Front Left, Front Right, Front Center, Low Frequency, Back Left, Back Right, Front Left of Center, Front Right of Center, Back Center, Side Left, Side Right, Top Center, Top Front Left, Top Front Center.

title: Audio Render CHOP
text: Audio Render CHOP (CHOP). Audio Render CHOP operator for TouchDesigner Parameters (15): Active, Listener Object COMP, Source Object COMP, Output Format, Binaural, Attenuation, Mapping Table, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio Spectrum CHOP
text: Audio Spectrum CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Spectrum CHOP calculates and displays the frequency spectrum of the input channels. Parameters (17): Mode, Visualization, FFT Size, 64, Frequency <-> Logarithmic Scaling, High Frequency Boost, Output Length, Match Length To Frequency, Set Output Length, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio Stream In CHOP
text: Audio Stream In CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Stream In CHOP can stream audio into TouchDesigner from any rtsp server, or from a WebRTC peer.  See Video Stream In TOP. Parameters (21): Source Type, Server, Server URL, Video Stream In TOP, Play, Open Timeout, Audio Sync Offset, Audio Sync Offset Unit, I, Volume, WebRTC DAT, WebRTC Connection, WebRTC Track, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio Stream Out CHOP
text: Audio Stream Out CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Stream Out CHOP can stream audio out to any rtsp client such as VideoLAN's VLC media player and Apple's Quicktime, or to a WebRTC peer. Parameters (16): Active, Mode, RTSP, Port, Stream Name, WebRTC, WebRTC Connection, WebRTC Track, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Audio VST CHOP
text: Audio VST CHOP (CHOP). The Audio VST CHOP loads VST3 Plugin files that generate (instruments) or process (filter) audio. See the VST overview. Parameters (29): File, Reload, Load Plugin State on Start, Sample Rate, Plugin GUI Always on Top, Display Plugin GUI, Learn Parameters, Regular Parameters, Read-Only Parameters, Clear Learned Parameters, Callbacks DAT, Custom Bus Layout, Output Bus Layout, Main Input Bus Layout, Aux Input, Enable, Bus Layout, Custom Playhead, Timecode Object/CHOP/DAT, Tempo (bpm), Signature, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match.

title: Audio Web Render CHOP
text: Audio Web Render CHOP (CHOP). The Audio Web Render CHOP plays the audio of a web page in a Web Render TOP.	In the Web Render TOP you need to have the Audio Options menus set to Route to Audio Web Render CHOPs, as well as the Active parameter on. Parameters (10): Active, Web Render TOP, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Base COMP
text: Base COMP (COMP). The Base Component has no panel parameters and no 3D object parameters. You would use it for a component that has no panel associated with it, nor any 3D, such as component that converted RGB channels to HSV channels. Parameters (24): Extension, Object, Name, Promote, Re-Init Extensions, Parent Shortcut, Global OP Shortcut, Internal OP, Shortcut, OP, Operator Viewer, Enable Cloning, Enable Cloning Pulse, Clone Master, Load on Demand, Enable External .tox, Enable External .tox Pulse, External .tox Path, Reload Custom Parameters, Reload Built-In Parameters, Save Backup of External, Sub-Component to Load, Relative File Path Behavior, Use Parent's Behavior.

title: Basis SOP
text: Basis SOP (SOP). The Basis SOP provides a set of operations applicable to the parametric space of spline curves and surfaces. The parametric space, also known as the "domain" of a NURBS or Bzier primitive, is defined by one basis in the U direction and, if the primitive is a surface, another basis in the V direction. The size of the domain is given by the values of the knots that make up the basis. Parameters (16): Group, Edit the U Basis, Parameterization, Unchanged, Knot Sequence, Read Basis, Range, Bias, Concatenate, Origin, Length, Scale, Raise to, Raise U to, Edit the V Basis, Raise V to.

title: Beat CHOP
text: Beat CHOP (CHOP). The Beat CHOP generates a variety of ramps, pulses and counters that are timed to the beats per minute and the sync as produced by the Beat Dialog. Parameters (36): Reference Operator, Use Reference Time Slice, Play Mode, Locked to Timeline, Period, Multiples, Shift Offset, Shift Step, Random Offset, Random Seed, Reset Condition, Off to On, Reset Bar Value, Wait after Reset, Reset, Reset Pulse, Update Global, Ramp, Pulse, Sine, Count, Count+Ramp, Bar, Beat.

title: Bind CHOP
text: Bind CHOP (CHOP). Allows for binding of CHOP channels and parameters. Parameters (12): Match by, Channel Number, Channel Pickup, Callbacks DAT, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: BlackTrax CHOP
text: BlackTrax CHOP (CHOP). License: Only available in TouchDesigner Pro. Parameters (24): Active, Port, Protocol, Messaging (UDP), Network Address, Sample Rate, Output Format, From Mapping Table, Max Beacons, Centroid, Velocity, Acceleration, LEDs, Reset Channels, Reset Pulse, Mapping Table, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Blend CHOP
text: Blend CHOP (CHOP). The Blend CHOP combines two or more CHOPs in input 2, 3 and so on, by using a set of blending channels in input 1. The blending channels cause different strengths of the CHOPs to contribute to the output of the CHOP. It works like the Blend SOP. Parameters (12): Method, Proportional, Omit First Weight Channel, Fix Underflows, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Blend COMP
text: Blend COMP (COMP). The Blend Component allows the blending of its attached 3D objects, allowing you to animate the parents of Components, sequencing (object A to object B to C, etc.), partial transformation inheritance, three-point orientation, and other effects. It gives you some extra flexibility in setting up parent-child relationships. It operates like the Switch and Sequence Blend SOPs insofar as it takes more than one input and blends or switches those into one output.	It affects the object transform only, not the contents of the blended components. Parameters (76): Type, Blend, Sequence, Reset Frame, Weight 1, Mask 1, Weight 2, Mask 2, Weight 3, Mask 3, Weight 4, Mask 4, Normal Offset, Orient Axes, Short Rotation, Transform Order, Scale Rotate Translate, Rotate Order, Rx Ry Rz, Translate, X, Rotate, Scale, Pivot.

title: Blend POP
text: Blend POP (POP). The Blend POP lets you blend together an attribute that occurs in all of it input POPs, such as the P attribute of all the inputs. There are at least 8 blend types. The simplest blend types will do operations like average an attribute of all the inputs, or take the maximum, minimum, sum or product of all the inputs for one attribute. The more complex blend types will mix based on a Blend Input factor (often a number between 0 and 1) for each input. You can specify more than one point attribute, more than one vertex and more than one primitive attribute to blend at the same time. You can get blend weights from attributes of the input POPs. The Blend POP's architecture for handling multiple inputs is similar to the [[Attribute Combine POP]]. '''Per-point mapping of parameters''' - The Random POP has a Map page, which allows every point to get a different value for the Blend Input weight parameter. In this mechanism, a separate attribute in the input contains values that override (or add to / multiply by) the parameter value. See [[Mapping POP Attributes to Parameters]]. Parameters (31): blendtype, differencing, lengthmismatchnotif, ignore, lengthmismatchaction, hold, weightchop, pointattrscope, *, primattrscope, vertattrscope, pointweight, primweight, vertweight, input, input0pop, input0weight, bypass, delinputattrs, parmcolorspace, srgb, parmreferencewhite, default, Blend Type.

title: Blend SOP
text: Blend SOP (SOP). The Blend SOP provides 3D metamorphosis between shapes with the same topology. It can blend between sixteen input SOPs using the average weight of each input's respective channel. It will also interpolate point colors and/or texture co-ordinates between shapes. Parameters (9): Group, Differencing, Blend Position, Blend Colors, Blend Normals, Blend Texture, Blend Up, Input, Weight.

title: Blob Track CHOP
text: Blob Track CHOP (CHOP). The Blob Track CHOP allows tracking blobs in 2D point data.
