title: Subdivide POP
text: Subdivide POP (POP). The Subdivide POP subdivides and smooths a surface. It divides each quad into two triangles, and then for each triangle subdivides it into 4 triangles cutting at the midpoint of each edge. The pre-existing and new points are blended with their neighbors to produce a more smooth surface. Increasing the Depth recurses several times in multiple passes, where the result of a pass is subdivided further in the next pass. Increasing the Crease Weight parameter toward 1 restores the original shape more, so this is a suitable method to simply subdivide faces as you may want with a Box POP. Parameters (14): iterations, creaseweight, simplecoeffs, cpureadback, bypass, delinputattrs, parmcolorspace, srgb, parmreferencewhite, default, Depth, Crease Weight, Simple Coefficients, Copy Topology Info Back to CPU.

title: Subdivide SOP
text: Subdivide SOP (SOP). The Subdivide SOP takes an input polygon surface (which can be piped into one or both inputs), and divides each face to create a smoothed polygon surface using a Catmull-Clark subdivision algorithm. Especially useful for avoiding the angular appearance often associated with polygonal models - without adding lots of extra geometry to the entire object. For best results, polygons should be convex and relatively uniform in distribution. Parameters (12): Group, Creases, Depth, Override Crease Weight Attribute, Crease Weight, Generate Resulting Creases, New Group, Close Cracks, Do Not Close, Surrounding Faces, No Edge Division, Bias.

title: Substance Select TOP
text: Substance Select TOP (TOP). The Substance Select TOP allows you to select a single texture out of the Substance TOP for further filtering and manipulation in TOPs. Parameters (19): Substance TOP, Output Channel, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes, Channel Mask, Pixel Format.

title: Substance TOP
text: Substance TOP (TOP). There is a tight integration between TouchDesigner and Allegorithmic (Adobe) Substance Designer, a material creation package that is also node-based and has extensive material libraries. The Substance TOP allows you to load .sbsar files from Substance Designer to use with the PBR Material. Parameters (24): Substance Designer File, Reload File, Graph, Output, Invert Normal Map, Engine, CPU, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes, Channel Mask, Pixel Format.

title: Substitute DAT
text: Substitute DAT (DAT). The Substitute DAT changes the cells of the incoming DAT using pattern matching and substitution strings. It outputs a table with the same number of rows and columns. Parameters (33): Before, After, Match, Anywhere, Case Sensitive, Expand the From String, Expand the To String, First Match Only, Exclude First Row, Exclude First Col, Select Rows, All, Start Row Name, Start Row Index, End Row Name, End Row Index, Row Select Values, Row Select Condition, From Column, Select Cols, Start Col Name, Start Col Index, End Col Name, End Col Index.

title: Subtract TOP
text: Subtract TOP (TOP). The Subtract TOP composites the input images together by subtracting the pixel values. Parameters (34): Fixed Layer, Input 1, Pre-Fit Overlay, Fill, Justify Horizontal, Left, Justify Vertical, Bottom, Extend Overlay, Hold, Rotate, Translate, X, Translate Units, Scale, Pivot, Pivot Units, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect.

title: Superquad SOP
text: Superquad SOP (SOP). The Superquad SOP generates an isoquadric surface. This produces a spherical shape that is similar to a metaball, with the difference that it doesn't change it's shape in response to what surrounds it. You can change the XY Exponent of an isoquadric surface to define it to be more "squarish" or "starish" in shape. Also, an isoquadric surface is always defined as a polygonal or mesh type geometry. Parameters (23): Primitive Type, Polygon, Connectivity, Rows, Modify Bounds, Radius, X, Center, Reverse Anchors, Anchor U, Anchor V, Anchor W, Orientation, X Axis, Columns, XY Exponent, Z Exponent, Multiple Points per Pole, Cusp Polygons, Cusp Angle, Texture Coordinates, Off, Compute Normals.

title: Surfsect SOP
text: Surfsect SOP (SOP). The Surfsect SOP performs boolean operations with NURBS and Bezier surfaces, or only generates profiles where the surfaces intersect. The individual surfaces do not need to be solids (i.e. wrap in U and V), nor do they need to form a solid as a group (for example, you can cut a grid with a sphere). Parameters (17): Group A, Group B, 3D Tolerance, 2D Tolerance, Marching Steps, Operation, Union, Keep Inside A, Keep Inside B, Keep Outside A, Keep Outside B, Target, A, A Profiles Group, B Profiles Group, Avoid Already Trimmed-Out Parts, Join Profiles Created by Multiple Surfaces.

title: Sweep SOP
text: Sweep SOP (SOP). The Sweep SOP sweeps primitives in the Cross-section input along Backbone Source primitive(s), creating ribbon and tube-like shapes. The cross-section primitives are placed at each point of the backbone perpendicular to it. The Backbone Source can have one or several primitives. If there is more than one, Sweep will sweep the cross section along each one. Parameters (19): X-Section Group, Path Group, Reference Group, Cycle Type, All Primitives at Each Point, Angle Fix, Fix Flipping, Remove Coincident Points on Path, Aim at Reference Points, Use Vertex, Connection Vertex, Scale, Twist, Roll, Create Groups, Sweep Groups, Skin Output, Off, Fast Sweep.

title: Switch CHOP
text: Switch CHOP (CHOP). The Switch CHOP allows you to control the flow of channels through a CHOPnet. It selects one of the input CHOPs by index and copies it exactly. This is useful for selecting one of several "gestures" or actions. Only one input chop can be selected at a time. Parameters (10): First Input is Index, Index, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Switch DAT
text: Switch DAT (DAT). The Switch DAT is a multi-input operator which lets you choose which input is output by using the Input parameter. Parameters (7): Index, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: Switch MAT
text: Switch MAT (MAT). The Switch MAT allows you to switch between multiple materials. Parameters (32): Index, Deform, Get Bone Data:, From a SOP, SOP with Capture Data, pCaptPath Attrib, pCaptData Attrib, Skeleton Root Path, MAT, Blending(Transparency), Source Color *, Zero, Destination Color *, One, Separate Alpha Function, Source Alpha *, Destination Alpha *, Depth Test, Depth Test Function, Less Than, Write Depth Values, Discard Pixels Based on Alpha, Keep Pixels with Alpha, Alpha Threshold.

title: Switch POP
text: Switch POP (POP). The Switch POP lets you choose one of the inputs, and outputs the input unchanged. The number of points, vertices and primitives of all of the inputs can be different. It uses a numeric parameter, Index, to choose the input, with 0 referring to the first input. If Index is fractional - a non-integer, it can blend two inputs: If the inputs have the same number of points, vertices and primitives, they can be blended, two adjacent inputs at a time. Tip: Since it blends only adjacent inputs (input N with input N+1 or N-1), to blend from the last input to the first input, attach the first input again as the last input, and use CHOPs to loop from 0 to number-of-inputs snap back to 0 when Index reaches number-of-inputs. If Index is out of range, it clamps. See the Blend POP to blend arbitrary combinations of inputs. The Switch POP's architecture for handling multiple inputs is similar to the [[Attribute Combine POP]]. Parameters (26): index, blend, lengthmismatchnotif, ignore, lengthmismatchaction, hold, pointattrscope, *, primattrscope, vertattrscope, input, input0pop, bypass, delinputattrs, parmcolorspace, srgb, parmreferencewhite, default, Index, Extend, Blend between Inputs, Length Mismatch, Point Attribute Scope, Primitive Attribute Scope.

title: Switch SOP
text: Switch SOP (SOP). The Switch SOP switches between up to 9999 possible inputs. The output of this SOP is specified by the Select Input field. This is useful for switching the output between several inputs depending on the evaluation of an expression placed in the Select Input field. Parameters (1): Select Input.

title: Switch TOP
text: Switch TOP (TOP). The Switch TOP is a multi-input operator which lets you switch which input is passed through using the Input parameter. Parameters (19): Index, Blend between Inputs, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes, Channel Mask, Pixel Format. Tips: Use fractional index values for cross-fading between inputs (e.g., 0.5 blends input 0 and input 1). Wire a CHOP channel to the Index parameter for dynamic switching. Great for A/B comparisons and live performance switching. Warnings: All inputs cook every frame regardless of which is selected, unless you use the 'Cook' parameter to limit this.

title: Sync In CHOP
text: Sync In CHOP (CHOP). License: Only available in TouchDesigner Pro. Parameters (12): Active, Multicast Address, Network Port, Timeout (msec), Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

Parameters (19): Use Spout Active Sender, Sender Name, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes, Channel Mask, Pixel Format.

text: Syphon Spout Out TOP (TOP). Note: For Spout on Windows, an Nvidia or AMD GPU is required, Intel does not work. Parameters (19): Active, Sender Name, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes, Channel Mask, Pixel Format.

title: Table COMP
text: Table COMP (COMP). The Table Component creates a grid of user interface gadgets. These panels are laid out in a grid format where the contents of each cell are defined by DAT tables. Parameters (148): Cell Attributes DAT, Row Attributes DAT, Col Attributes DAT, Table Attributes DAT, Values DAT, Table Rows, Table Columns, Table Align, Layout Horizontal Left to Right, Font Size Unit, Pixels, Info Format, State Column, Table Offset, X, Table Reset, Y, Width, Height, Fixed Aspect, Off, Aspect Ratio, Depth Layer, Horizontal Mode.

text: Table DAT (DAT). Table DAT operator for TouchDesigner Parameters (22): Edit.., File, Sync to File, Default Read Encoding, Auto Detect, Load on Start, Write on Toe Save, Remove Blank Lines, Fill Type, Manual, Rows, Columns, Cell Expression, Include Names, Fills, Names, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: Tablet CHOP
text: Tablet CHOP (CHOP). The Tablet CHOP gets the Wacom tablet X and Y values, and also gets pen tip pressure, X tilt and Y tilt, and the various pen buttons. Two pens can be used to output one set of channels for each pen. The Tablet CHOP will initialize/install when the CHOP is created. Parameters (28): X Coordinate, Y Coordinate, Pressure, X Tilt, Y Tilt, Finger Wheel, Thumb Wheel, Rotation, Button 1, Button 2, Button 3, Button 4, Button 5, Active, Off, Sample Rate, Extend Left, Hold, Extend Right, Default Value, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate.

title: Text COMP
text: Text COMP (COMP). The Text COMP is a panel used to display text for building user interfaces. The text can be view-only, view-only but copy/pasteable, or be fully editable directly in the panel. Parameters (190): Mode, Text, Specification DAT, Specification CHOP, Type, String, Formatting, Default, Custom Formatting, Precision, Thousands Separator, None, Framerate, Use Smart Punctuation, Formatting Codes, Smart Punctuation, Word Wrap, Edit Mode, Locked, Shift-Enter for New Line, Reading Direction, Left To Right, Drag Drop Behaviour, Use Panel System.

title: Text DAT
text: Text DAT (DAT). The Text DAT lets you edit free-form, multi-line ASCII text. It is used for scripts, GLSL shaders, notes, XML and other purposes. "Free-form text" is one of the two forms of DATs (the other being tables of rows and columns of cells, each cell containing a text string as in a Table DAT). Parameters (13): Edit.., File, Sync to File, Load on Start, Load File, Write on Toe Save, Write File, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap. Tips: Text DAT is the primary container for Python scripts, GLSL shaders, and any text content. Use the edit callback to run code whenever the text is modified. Right-click and select 'Run Script' to execute Python code in a Text DAT.

title: Text SOP
text: Text SOP (SOP). The Text SOP creates text geometry from any TrueType or OpenType font that is installed on the system, or any TrueType/OpenType font file on disk. Unicode is supported. Parameters (31): Font, Font File, Bold, Italic, Font Size X, Font Size Y, Keep Font Ratio, Scale Font to BBox Height, Output, Triangles, Level of Detail, Language, Reading Direction, Left To Right, Kerning, Line Spacing, Horizontal Align, In Reading Direction, Word Wrap, Word Wrap Size, Text, Legacy Parsing, Transform Order, Scale Rotate Translate.

title: Text TOP
text: Text TOP (TOP). The Text TOP displays text strings in an image. It allows for multiple fonts, sizes, colors, borders, character separation and line separation. The text can be displayed as bit maps, anti-aliased lines, or filled polygon characters. Any TrueType font can be rendered by the Text TOP. Unicode is supported.	The new Scalable Display Method uses Slug Library to render scalable, resolution independent text at the highest qulaity available in the Text TOP. Parameters (89): Field Component, DAT, DAT Row, DAT Col, Specification DAT, Text, Legacy Parsing, Append Value, Value, Total Digits, Decimal Digits, Post Text, CHOP Value %-Replace, CHOP, Word Wrap, Smart Quote, Font, Font File, Character Set, Unicode, Display Method, Automatic, Anti-Alias, 1x (Off).

title: Texture 3D TOP
text: Texture 3D TOP (TOP). The Texture 3D TOP creates a 3D texture map. It saves a series of images in one array of pixels. This TOP can be used with Time Machine TOP, as well as materials. Using materials, a particular image in this array can be accessed by specifying a W texture coordinate, while the U and V coordinates are used to specify a texture location on that image. Refer to the 3D Texture article for more information. Parameters (29): Type, 3D Texture, Active, Replace Single, Replace Single Pulse, Replace Index, Pre Fill, Pre Fill Pulse, Cache Size, Step Size, Reset, Reset Pulse, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel.

title: Texture Map POP
text: Texture Map POP (POP). The Texture Map POP creates the Tex attribute which is used to apply texture maps to surfaces, or to position procedurally-generated textures to surfaces. The Tex attribute is a float3 3-component attribute. It can appear as a point attribute or a vertex attribute. It is created by all the generator POPs like the [[Plane POP]], [[Grid POP]], [[Sphere POP]], [[Rectangle POP]], [[Box POP]] etc., but Tex can also created with the Texture Map POP. There are various projection methods, each useful depending on the type of geometry it is being applied to and its use-case. Equirectangular Inside and Outside is a spherical polar longitude/latitude mapping used on vertices, used in VR, either to be viewed from the inside of a sphere or outside. Domes require the mapping type Equidistant Azimuth, a fish eye projection. Projection mapping uses perspective from camera simulating what is "seen" from the position of a projector. Triplanar Coordinates is a type that generates 3 sets of texture coordinates (3 attributes) used to map 1 texture onto an object but is a blend of 3 orthographic projections onto 3 sides of an object. There is nothing special about the Tex attribute - any attribute can be used by a Material. The [[Normalize POP]] and the [[Projection POP]] produce attributes that can be used with a material, like doing orhtographic scaled projections, or spherical/cylindrical projections. Some materials require multiple texture coordinates, which can be created with any of these methods in creating Tex2, Tex3, etc. and provided to the materials. Note: In SOPs this was similar to the use of the uvw attribute for texture coordinates. See also [[PBR MAT]], [[Phong MAT]], [[Normalize POP]] and the [[Projection POP]] Parameters (73): Tex, group, transforminput, inputtexattr, type, xyznorm, posattr, axis, x, fov, camera, cameraaspect, cameraaspectx, applyto, natural, s, su, offset, offsetu, angle, fixseams, outputattrscope, P, overrideautoattr.

title: Texture SOP
text: Texture SOP (SOP). The Texture SOP assigns texture UV and W coordinates to the Source geometry for use in texture and bump mapping. It generates multi-layers of texture coordinates. Parameters (20): Primitive Group, Texture Layer, Texture Type, Orthographic, Projection Axis, X Axis, Camera Name, Apply to, Natural location, Scale, Offset, Rotate, Fix Face Seams, Transform Order, Scale Rotate Translate, Rotate Order, Rx Ry Rz, Translate, X, Pivot.

title: Threshold TOP
text: Threshold TOP (TOP). The Threshold TOP creates a matte with pixel values set to 0 for pixels below the threshold value, and 1 for pixels greater than or equal to the threshold value. Parameters (25): Comparator, Less, RGB, Luminance, Threshold, Alpha, Same as RGB, Soften, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes, Channel Mask.

title: Tile TOP
text: Tile TOP (TOP). The Tile TOP tiles images in a repeating pattern. It also has a Crop option which crops an image by defining the position of the left, right, bottom, and top edges of the image. Parameters (38): Crop Left, Crop Left Unit, Crop Right, Crop Right Unit, Crop Bottom, Crop Bottom Unit, Crop Top, Crop Top Unit, Extend, Hold, Transpose, Repeat X, Repeat Y, Flip X, Flip Y, Reflect X, Reflect Y, Overlap U, Overlap U Unit, Overlap V, Overlap V Unit, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution.

title: Time COMP
text: Time COMP (COMP). The Time Component allows each component to have its own timeline (clock). The Time Component contains a network of operators that can drive a Timeline, drive animations in Animation COMPs, or be used to drive any custom time-based system. The Time Component's parameters define the speed, range, various options for the time system. Parameters (36): Play, Rate, Start, End, Range Limit, Loop, Range Start, Range End, Reset Frame, Signature, Tempo, Run Independently, Extension, Object, Name, Promote, Re-Init Extensions, Parent Shortcut, Global OP Shortcut, Internal OP, Shortcut, OP, Operator Viewer, Enable Cloning.

title: Time Machine TOP
text: Time Machine TOP (TOP). The Time Machine TOP combines pixels in a sequence of images stored in a Texture 3D TOP. Whereas "morphing" warps an image "spatially" (in xy), Time Machine warps images only in time. Parameters (21): Black Offset, Black Offset Unit, White Offset, White Offset Unit, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes, Channel Mask, Pixel Format.

title: Time Slice CHOP
text: Time Slice CHOP (CHOP). The Time Slice CHOP outputs a time slice of samples. It is used to generate smooth in-betweens when TouchDesigner cannot cook/draw fast enough and keep up with the animation's frames per second. When you send it to a Record CHOP or Gesture CHOP, you will see the channels recorded and playing back more smoothly. Parameters (11): Method, Hold, Quaternion Blend, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Timecode CHOP
text: Timecode CHOP (CHOP). The Timecode CHOP generates Timecode data (channels, a .timecode python object and other python members). Its Mode menu provides a variety of ways to get, set and generate timecode through its parameters, including being driven from a timecode string, a set of channels, or other OPs that have timecode such as video input devices. You can also set timecode with python functions. Parameters (41): SMPTE, Mode, Locked to Timeline, Rate, Drop Frame, Off, Index, Index Unit, I, Timecode String, Frame, Second, Minute, Hour, Initialize, Start, Play, Timecode Object, CHOP, OP, Custom Length, Length, Length Units, Cycle.

title: Timeline CHOP
text: Timeline CHOP (CHOP). The Timeline CHOP outputs time-based CHOP channels for a specific component. The time channels are defined by a Time Component whose Path can be determined using the python expression me.time. When a Reference Node is specified, the time defined at that node is used.  When the Reference Node is not specified, then the time defined at the Timeline CHOP's location is used (ie. time defined at me.time). Parameters (21): Reference Operator, Use Timecode, Timecode Object/CHOP/DAT, Frame, Rate, Start, End, Range Start, Range End, Signature 1, Signature 2, BPM, Play, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Timer CHOP
text: Timer CHOP (CHOP). The Timer CHOP is an engine for running timed processes. It outputs channels such as timing fractions, counters, pulses and timer states, and it calls python functions (callbacks) when various timing events occur. Parameters (89): Active, Never, Time Control, Lock To Timeline, Defer Par Changes, Initialize, Start, Length Type, Fixed, Length, Length Units, I, Delay, Delay Units, Play, Speed, Cue, Cue Pulse, Cue Point, Cue Units, Cycle, Cycle Limit, Maximum Cycles, Cycle End Alert.

title: TOP to CHOP
text: TOP to CHOP (TOP). The TOP to CHOP converts pixels in a TOP image to CHOP channels. Each pixel color element (RGBA) is placed in a separate channel. Depending on the parameter options, the node will either create a single set of channels for the whole image or it can create a separate set of channels for each scanline (row). Parameters (43): TOP, Download Type, Immediate (Slow), Red, Green, Blue, Alpha, Output as Single Channel Set, Exclude NaNs, Active Channel, None, RGBA Units, 0 to 1, Crop, Pixel (U,V), UV Units, U Start, U End, V Start, V End, Interpolate, Nearest Sample, Image Left, Hold.

title: TOP to POP
text: TOP to POP (POP). Position and Active is a shortcut for P in RGB, and alpha is set to 1 for active pixels (pixels with a valid attribute value). The First RGBA Contains menu lets you treat the RGBA in different ways, including as depth maps, point clouds, height maps. Using Custom you specify which attribute and components you expect in the RGBA of the TOP, so max 4 could be P(0) Color(2) N(1) P(2). When you Use Dimension all pixels are active since the resolution matches the TOP When using the other modes you can end up with unused pixels (you have 40 points, fit to square gives you 7x7, so you end up with 9 unused pixels), alpha is only set to 1 for used pixels. Parameters (98): P(0) Color(2) N(1) P(2), rgba, color, maxpointsenable, maxpoints, input, input0top, input0chanscope, r, input0attrscope, P, input0filter, nearest, attr, attr0name, custom, attr0customname, attr0type, float, attr0numcomps, 1, attr0defvalue, attr0defvalue1, surftype.

title: Topology POP
text: Topology POP (POP). The Topology POP gives finer control on how to combine existing points and topology (vertices and primitives), as well as their memory allocation. Any unsigned int attribute from the input POP or another POP, interpreted as a list of point indices, can be used to describe the topology of the POP, alongside a manual description of the number of primitives (Topology Info), either using the parameters when the number of primitives of each type are known on the CPU, or using values coming from attributes, when the number of primitives of each type are only known on the GPU. This allows to combine multiple buffers created with GLSL and GLSL Advanced POP to describe geometry for example. Alternatively the Topology and Topology info can be provided from another POP. Primitives and Vertices attributes can also be merged in from other POPs. Last but not least, for POPs whose numbers of points and primitives are known on the GPU, the maximum numbers can be updated here, if the user knows them to be smaller, to reduce the memory allocation of downstream POPs. Alternatively if the exact counts are known they can also be entered manually and the POP updated to know that its point count info and topology info are now known on the CPU, without incurring the cost and stall of a download. (Refer to Learning about POPs, Information known on CPU/GPU) Parameters (107): render, maxpointsmode, input, maxpoints, primsourcemode, primmode, topo, primspop, pointindexattrclass, point, pointindexattrname, vertattrmode, none, vertattrpop, vertattrclass, primattrmode, primattrpop, primattrclass, topology, ref, maxtrianglesmode, primsource, maxtriangles, maxquadsmode.

title: Torus POP
text: Torus POP (POP). The Torus POP creates rows and columns of points in a closed tube shape. The points can be connected with a variety of primitives using the Connectivity menu. The ring and the cross-section of the torus each have a radius parameter. The Connectivity menu lets you output triangles, alternating triangles, quads, lines, line strips, point primitives or no primitives (None, only points). You specify number of rows and columns. Columns refers to the divisions around the ring, Rows to the divisions around the cross-section circle. In the Connectivity menu you can create lines and line strips along Rows, Columns or both merged together. The torus is symmetric around an axis, Y by default, but the torus can be post-rotated and post-translated using the Rotate and Translate parameters. Anchor will line up the left, center or right side of the torus to X=0, Y=0 or Z=0. The Details page lets you make a partial torus by specifying an arc of the ring with any starting/ending angle. The End Caps toggle will add triangles or quads to fill the end caps and make the torus a closed volume, which still preserves the [[Dimension]] since no points are added or removed, only primitives are added. [[Dimension]]: When the torus is passed to other POPs it cannot be known for certain how many rows and columns were specified. However the Torus POP (and other generator POPs) output its Dimensions as metadata to all POPs connected to it, indicating its organizations of points in the points list. A torus has dimensions numCols numRows, which can be used by built-in attributes like _Dim[] or by GLSL code. You can output point or vertex normals (creating the N attribute), and you can output point or vertex texture coordinates (Tex attribute). The Torus POP takes an input which will cause the starting tube to have the same bounding box as the bounding box of the input. Then turning on Modify Bounds lets you further transform the torus. See also [[Tube POP]], [[Sphere POP]], [[Grid POP]], [[Field POP]], [[Point Generator POP]], [[Revolve POP]], [[Dimension]] Parameters (52): _Dim[], surftype, none, linetype, linestrip, orient, x, modifybounds, rad, radx, cols, rows, anchoru, anchorv, anchorw, t, tx, r, rx, texture, normal, closedu, closedv, angleu.

title: Torus SOP
text: Torus SOP (SOP). The Torus SOP generates complete or specific sections of torus shapes (like a doughnut). Parameters (28): Primitive Type, Polygon, Connectivity, Rows, Orientation, X Axis, Modify Bounds, Radius, X, Center, Reverse Anchors, Anchor U, Anchor V, Anchor W, Columns, Angle Offset, Imperfect, U Order, V Order, U Angle, V Angle, U Wrap, V Wrap, U End Caps.

title: Touch In CHOP
text: Touch In CHOP (CHOP). The Touch In CHOP can be used to create a high speed connection between two TouchDesigner processes via CHOPs. Parameters (24): Protocol, Streaming (TCP/IP), Address, Network Port, Active, Queue Target, Queue Target Unit, Queue Variance, Queue Variance Unit, Maximum Queue, Max Queue Unit, Queue Adjust Time, Adjust Unit, Recover Outside Range, Use Synced Ports, Off, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Touch In DAT
text: Touch In DAT (DAT). The Touch In DAT receives full tables across the network from the Touch Out DAT, as opposed to messages with the other network based DATs. Parameters (12): Protocol, Streaming (TCP/IP), Network Address, Port, Shared Connection, Active, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: Touch In TOP
text: Touch In TOP (TOP). The Touch In TOP will read in image data send over a TCP/IP network connection from a Touch Out TOP. The other TouchDesigner process can be on the same computer or from another computer anywhere on the connected network. Parameters (24): Computer Name / IP, Network Port, Active, Minimum Target, Maximum Target, Maximum Queue, Queue Adjust Time, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes, Channel Mask, Pixel Format.

title: Touch Out CHOP
text: Touch Out CHOP (CHOP). The Touch Out CHOP can be used to create high speed connection between two TouchDesigner processes. Data is sent over TCP/IP. The Touch Out CHOP (server) sends it's data to a Touch In CHOP (client). The Touch Out CHOP is similar to a Pipe Out CHOP but highly optimized for TouchDesigner-to-TouchDesigner communication. For interfacing with other software or devices, see the Pipe Out CHOP. Parameters (19): Protocol, Streaming (TCP/IP), Address, Network Port, Active, Max Queue Size, Max Queue Size Unit, Cook Every Frame, Resend Names, Use Synced Ports, Off, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Touch Out DAT
text: Touch Out DAT (DAT). The Touch Out DAT sends full DAT tables across the network to the Touch In DAT in another TouchDesigner process, as opposed to messages with the other network based DATs. It is an extremely powerful mechanism to sync data between TouchDesigner processes. Parameters (14): Protocol, Streaming (TCP/IP), Network Address, Port, Shared Connection, Active, Redundant Sends, Resend Data, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: Touch Out TOP
text: Touch Out TOP (TOP). The Touch Out TOP sends a TOP image stream over TCP/IP to a Touch In TOP. The Touch In TOP can be in another TouchDesigner session on the same computer or on a computer anywhere on the connected network. Parameters (23): Network Port, Active, FPS, Video Codec, Uncompressed, Cook Always, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes, Channel Mask, Pixel Format.

title: Trace SOP
text: Trace SOP (SOP). The Trace SOP reads an image file and automatically traces it, generating a set of faces around areas exceeding a certain brightness threshold. You can control this threshold and the resolution of the resulting faces. Parameters (15): TOP Name, Threshold, Add Point Texture, Remove Borders, Compute Normals, Border Width, Resample Shapes, Step Size, Smooth Shapes, Corner Delta, Fit to Curves, Fitting Error, Convert to Poly, Level of Detail, Hole Faces.

title: Trail CHOP
text: Trail CHOP (CHOP). The Trail CHOP displays a history of its input channels back in time. A window of time is displayed from the current frame back in time, the size of this window is set by the Window Length parameter. The last sample in the Trail CHOP is a sample of the input at the current frame. Note: If the input channels change in number or names, the Trail CHOP clears its trails and begins anew.  You can use a Replace CHOP in this case to create stand in values. Parameters (18): Active, Grow Length, Window Length, Window Length Unit, Capture, Time Slice, Resample, Rate, Sample Rate, Reset, Reset Pulse, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Trail POP
text: Trail POP (POP). The Trail POP captures and retains all the points of the input for the most recent N frames or N "slices", being a time-history of the input's points. It then optionally creates primitives that connects the points in various ways. If the number of points of the input is the same for all the slices, then it can easily skin points together using the Connectivity menu, such as maling a line strip for each point over time. If the number of points is changing per-frame, as it would with a particle system, it can still connect time-related dots by using an Id attribute on the points, and making sure the Id of a particle stays the same through its life. You can select which attribute it co-relates to using the Match by Attribute parameter. It ignores and does not use the connectivity (primitives) of the input. If the number of points of the input is the same for all the slices, it adds one new [[Dimension]] whose size is the number of slices. Trail Length can be expressed in Seconds or Frames. Seconds is preferable as it will remain the same if you move the Trail POP to another project with a different frame rate. Trail Increment lets you skip frames - capture a slice every Mth frame. Reset clears and restarts the trail. Oldest Point First reverses the order of the slices. The Trail POP deals with frame drops elegantly by (optionally) filling in data for missing frames and creating stand-in slices, and (optionally) tagging each slice with an Age attribute. This can give smoother frame-drop-resistant trail curves. Parameters (51): Id, active, alwayscook, length, lengthunit, seconds, inc, incunit, reset, resetpulse, ageattr, none, oldestpointfirst, fillmissedframes, attrmatch, attrname, uintmax, uint4, maxls, surftype, closed, t, tx, r.

title: Trail SOP
text: Trail SOP (SOP). The Trail SOP takes an input SOP and makes a trail of each point of the input SOP over the past several frames, and connects the trails in different ways. It will generate trails of any input geometry, whether it is a cube translating, a deforming surface, or particles. This is useful for multi-frame ghosting effects and temporal modelling. Parameters (12): Result Type, Preserve Original, Trail Length, Trail Increment, Cache Size, Evaluate Within Frame Range, Connectivity, Rows, Close Rows, Velocity Scale, Reset, Reset Pulse.

title: Transform CHOP
text: Transform CHOP (CHOP). The Transform CHOP takes transformations in various formats, applied operations to them, and outputs them in various formats. It can be used to: Parameters (45): Custom Input Orders, Transform Order, Scale Rotate Translate, Rotate Order, Rx Ry Rz, Input 0 Pre Operation, None, Input 1 Pre Operation, Input Operation, Input 0, then Input 1, Input Weight, Translate, X, Rotate, Scale, Pivot, Xform Matrix/CHOP/DAT, Multiply Order, Input, then Transform Page, Look At, Up Vector, Forward Direction, +X, Pre Operation.

title: Transform POP
text: Transform POP (POP). The Transform POP applies a translate, rotate and scale to the position (attribute P) and normal (attribute N) of all the points of the input. It can apply the transform to any float3 attribute, not just P, and then output to any existing or new float3 attribute, by default P. It can apply the transform to point, vertex or primitive attributes. It can apply partial transforms to the points based on a 0 to 1 Weight attribute so that some parts of the geometry are affected and other parts not transformed. The [[Field POP]] can generate a Weight attribute to be used by Transform. '''Per-point mapping of parameters''' - using the Map page, every point can receive a different value for the transform parameters, based on mapping incoming attributes to the translate, scale, rotate, pivot etc. parameters, So essentially every point can get a completely different transform. See [[Mapping POP Attributes to Parameters]]. A further transform can be applied by specifying a Look At component (typically a [[Null COMP]] or [[Geometry COMP]]), and choosing which axis points to the origin of that component. Another transform may be applied by providing a 16-value transform matrix via a CHOP or DAT. The order of applying the translate, rotate and scale parameters is determined by the Transform Order and the Rotate Order menus. The Inverse toggle parameter applies an inverse of the transform specified. (Instead of tx=2 ry=50 sz=4, the equivalent of sz=.25, ry=-50 tx=-2) On the Align page there are numerous options for aligning the X, Y or Z coordinate to the different sides/center of the input's bounding box or to the bounding box of a reference POP. See also [[Math Mix POP]], [[Trig POP]] Parameters (103): P, mode, transformgeo, attrclass, point, inputattrscope, group, weightattr, xord, srt, rord, xyz, t, tx, r, rx, s, sx, p, px, scale, invert, vlength, lookat.

title: Transform SOP
text: Transform SOP (SOP). The Transform SOP translates, rotates and scales the input geometry in "object space" or local to the SOP. The Model Editor and the Transform SOP both work in "object space", and change the X Y Z positions of the points. In contrast, animating the transformation channels of an object in the Geometry Viewer Pane moves/scales the entire object in "world space" and does not affect the XYZ point positions of the geometry. Parameters (30): Group, Transform Order, Scale Rotate Translate, Rotate Order, Rx Ry Rz, Translate, X, Rotate, Scale, Pivot, Uniform Scale, Normals Maintain Length, Look At, Up Vector, Forward Direction, +X, Post Transform Order, Scale Translate, Post Translate X, Off, From Input, Min, To Reference, Post Translate Y.

title: Transform TOP
text: Transform TOP (TOP). The Transform TOP applies 2D transformations to a TOP image like translate, scale, rotate, and multi-repeat tiling. The background can be filled with solid color and alpha. Parameters (37): Transform Order, Scale Rotate Translate, Translate, X, Translate Unit, Rotate, Scale, Grow / Shrink, Pivot, Pivot Unit, Backgound Color, Red, Pre-Multiply RGB by Alpha, Comp Over Background Color, Mipmap Bias, Extend, Hold, Limit Tiles, Tile U, Tile V, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W. Tips: Use Extend mode to control what happens outside the transformed area (hold, zero, repeat, mirror). Combine translate, rotate, and scale for complex 2D transformations. The Pivot parameter sets the center of rotation and scale operations.

title: Transform XYZ CHOP
text: Transform XYZ CHOP (CHOP). The Transform XYZ CHOP is used to transform positions and vector. The difference between transforming a position vs. a vector is that a vector won't have the translation portion of the transformation applied to it.The Transform XYZ CHOP first groups the channels from the first input by looking for 'x', 'y' and 'z' as the last character in the channel names. Then it treats each of the sets created as either a Position or a Vector depending on the parameter choice. The second input can be connected, and must describe a transform in the same format of channels that the Transform CHOP supports. The second input is combined with the 'Transform' page parameters, and the resulting transform is applied to the input positions and vectors.CHOPs with multiple-samples can be provided, which allows for larger amount of positional data to be transformed in a single CHOP. Parameters (28): Input 0 Type, Position, Normalize, Custom Input Order, Transform Order, Scale Rotate Translate, Rotate Order, Rx Ry Rz, Input 1 Pre Operation, None, Translate, X, Rotate, Scale, Pivot, Pre Operation, Multiply Order, Input, then Transform Page, Un-matched Channels, Warn, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate.

title: Transpose DAT
text: Transpose DAT (DAT). The Transpose DAT converts rows into columns. The number of rows becomes the number of columns, and vice versa. Parameters (6): Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: Trig POP
text: Trig POP (POP). The Trig POP is a simple operator that converts attributes that are angles into the trigonometry representation of angles (sine, cosine, tangent). Alternately, the Trig POP can convert from the sine, cosine, tangent representation back to angles using the asin, acos and atan functions. asin(x) can be thought of as "the angle whose sine value is x". You can choose the units your angles are expressed in - degrees (360 per rotation), radians (2 * 3.14157 per rotation) or simply rotations (1 means 1 rotation) For example if an attribute has a value of 30 (degrees) and the Operation is set to sin(A), the Trig POP will output .5 to a new attribute. sinh, cosh and tanh are the hyperbolic functions. This POP does the same thing as the sin, cos... , asin, acos ... functions in the [[Math Mix POP]], [[Math Combine POP]], [[Math POP]]. where you can also choose the units of your angles. sin and cos are true "functions" because for every value of angle A, there is one and only one value for sin(A). However asin(A) etc are not functions as values of A may have an infinite number or zero numbers of values for asin(A)). The function atan2(A,B) requires that you provide two attribute values. Note: the other trig functions not included are cot(x) is equal to 1/tan(x). sec(x) = 1/cos(x), and csc(x) = 1/sin(x). See also [[Math Mix POP]], [[LFO CHOP]] Parameters (33): attrclass, point, group, inputattrscope, inputattrscopeb, oper, none, fromunit, deg, tounit, outputattrscope, P, overrideautoattr, newattrtype, float, attrnumcomps, 1, newattrdefaultval, newattrdefaultval1, bypass, delinputattrs, parmcolorspace, srgb, parmreferencewhite.

title: Trigger CHOP
text: Trigger CHOP (CHOP). The Trigger CHOP starts an audio-style attack/decay/sustain/release (ADSR) envelope to all trigger pulses in the input channels. A trigger point occurs whenever the first input's channel increases across the trigger threshold value. Most commonly, an ADSR starts when the input goes from 0 to 1. Parameters (46): Release = Trigger Threshold, Trigger Threshold, Release Threshold, Re-Trigger Delay, Re-Trigger Delay Unit, Min Trigger Length, Min Trigger Length Unit, Trigger On, Increasing Values, Multi Trigger, Ignore During Attack, Clamp at Peak Level, Trigger Pulse, Update Once per Cycle, Complete Envelope, Remainder, Discard Remainder, Delay Length, Delay Length Unit, Attack Length, Attack Length Unit, Attack Shape, Linear, Peak Level.

title: Trim CHOP
text: Trim CHOP (CHOP). The Trim CHOP shortens or lengthens the input's channels. A part of the interval can be preserved or removed. If the channels are being lengthened, the extend conditions of the channel will be used to get the new values. Parameters (16): Unit Values, Absolute, Start, Start Unit, End, End Unit, Discard, Exterior, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Trim SOP
text: Trim SOP (SOP). The Trim SOP cuts out parts of a spline surface, or uncuts previously cut pieces. When a portion of the surface is trimmed, it is not actually removed from the surface; instead, that part is made invisible. This means that you can still modify the surface (modify the position of its points, for instance) that is not displayed in order to affect the part that is displayed. Parameters (6): Group, Keep Outside, Process Profiles Individually, Build outer Trim-Loop Explicitly, Trimming Tolerance, Altitude.

title: Tristrip SOP
text: Tristrip SOP (SOP). The Tristrip SOP convert geometry into triangle strips. Triangle strips are faster to render than regular triangles or quads. Parameters (3): Source Group, Constrain Strip Length, Maximum Strip Length.

title: Tube POP
text: Tube POP (POP). The Tube POP creates rows and columns of points in a cylinder or tube shape. The points can be connected with a variety of primitives using the Connectivity menu. The bottom and top of the tube each have a radius parameter allowing for a tapered tube, in addition to the height parameter. The Connectivity menu lets you output triangles, alternating triangles, quads, lines, line strips, point primitives or no primitives (None, only points). You specify number of rows and columns. Columns refers to the divisions around the circles, Rows to the divisions along the sides. In the Connectivity menu you can create lines and line strips along Rows, Columns or both merged together. The tube is symmetric around an axis, Y by default, but the tube can be post-rotated and post-translated using the Rotate and Translate parameters. Anchor will line up the left, center or right side of the tube to X=0, Y=0 or Z=0. The Details page lets you make a partial cylinder by specifying arcs with any starting/ending angle. The End Caps toggle will add triangles or quads to fill the end caps and make the tube a closed volume, which still preserves the [[Dimension]] since no points are added or removed, only primitives are added. Dimension: When the tube is passed to other POPs it cannot be known for certain how many rows and columns were specified. However the Tube POP (and other generator POPs) output its [[Dimension]]s as metadata to all POPs connected to it, indicating its organizations of points in the points list. A tube has dimensions ''numCols numRows'', which can be used by built-in attributes like _Dim[] or by GLSL code. You can output point or vertex normals (creating the N attribute), and you can output point or vertex texture coordinates (Tex attribute). The Tube POP takes an input which will cause the starting tube to have the same bounding box as the bounding box of the input. Then turning on Modify Bounds lets you further transform the tube. See also [[Grid POP]], [[Sphere POP]], [[Torus POP]], [[Revolve POP]], [[Dimension]] Parameters (51): numCols numRows, surftype, none, linetype, linestrip, orient, x, modifybounds, rad, radx, height, cols, rows, anchoru, anchorv, anchorw, t, tx, r, rx, texture, normal, closedu, angleu.

title: Tube SOP
text: Tube SOP (SOP). The Tube SOP generates open or closed tubes, cones, or pyramids along the X, Y or Z axes. It outputs as meshes, polygons or simply a tube Primitive. Parameters (27): Primitive Type, Primitive, Connectivity, Rows, Orientation, X Axis, Orient Bounds, Modify Bounds, Rotate Order, Rx Ry Rz, Center, X, Rotate, Radius, Height, Reverse Anchors, Anchor U, Anchor V, Anchor W, Imperfect, Columns, U Order, V Order, End Caps.

title: TUIO In DAT
text: TUIO In DAT (DAT). The TUIO In DAT receives and parses TUIO messages (received over network) into columns in the table. TUIO packets OSC bundles, so TUIO data can also be viewed in its more raw form in an OSC In DAT.	It currently uses the TUIO 1.1 protocol. The TUIO 2.0 protocol is not yet supported. Parameters (16): Protocol, Messaging (UDP), Network Address, Port, Shared Connection, Active, Callbacks DAT, Execute from, Current Node, From Operator, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: Twist POP
text: Twist POP (POP). The Twist POP performs non-linear deformations on points such as Twist, Bend, Shear, Taper, Linear Taper, and Squash & Stretch. You set up one or two axes (X, Y or Z) and a pivot point to determine the direction and center of deformation. The amount of deformation is set by the Strength parameter, and the rolloff away from the pivot point and/or axes is affected by the Rolloff parameter. The amount of deformation can also be increased or decreased using a Weight attribute, which multiplies the Strength per-point. You can deform any point, vertex or primitive attribute. Parameters (38): attrclass, point, inputattrscope, weightattr, op, twist, paxis, x, saxis, p, px, strength, pivot, outputattrscope, P, overrideautoattr, newattrtype, float, attrnumcomps, 1, newattrdefaultval, newattrdefaultval1, bypass, delinputattrs.

title: Twist SOP
text: Twist SOP (SOP). The Twist SOP performs non-linear deformations such as bend, linear taper, shear, squash and stretch, taper and twist. Each deformation will distort the object in one or more axes. Parameters (10): Group, Operation, Twist, Primary Axis, X Axis, Secondary Axis, Pivot, X, Strength, Rolloff.

title: UDP In DAT
text: UDP In DAT (DAT). The UDP In DAT is used for receiving information over a UDP connection between two remotely located computers. It captures all the messages without any queuing or buffering, and allows you to send it any messages you want. Once this DAT has received a message it can reply to the sender using the 'send' command. Using the send command before it has received a message will not work because it doesn't know where to send data yet. When in multicast mode it doesn't send out a multicast message, it sends a reply directly to the originator for the last multicast message it got. Parameters (22): Protocol, Messaging (UDP), Network Address, Port, Shared Connection, Row/Callback Format, One Per Byte, Active, Callbacks DAT, Execute from, Current Node, From Operator, Clamp Output, Maximum Lines, Clear Output, Bytes Column, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: UDP Out DAT
text: UDP Out DAT (DAT). The UDP Out DAT is used to send information over a UDP connection to/from a remotely-located computer. Use the sendBytes() or send() methods of the udpoutDAT_Class to send messages. Parameters (26): Active, Protocol, Messaging (UDP), Network Address, Port, Shared Connection, Row/Callback Format, One Per Byte, Local Address, Local Port Mode, Automatic, Local Port, Callbacks DAT, Execute from, Current Node, From Operator, Clamp Output, Maximum Lines, Clear Output, Bytes Column, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat.

title: Under TOP
text: Under TOP (TOP). The Under TOP places Input1 'under' Input2. The alpha of Input2 is used to determine what parts of the Input1 image are visible in the result. Parameters (34): Fixed Layer, Input 1, Pre-Fit Overlay, Fill, Justify Horizontal, Left, Justify Vertical, Bottom, Extend Overlay, Hold, Rotate, Translate, X, Translate Units, Scale, Pivot, Pivot Units, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect.

title: USD COMP
text: USD COMP (COMP). The USD COMP loads and imports most geometric schemas from a USD file in crate/binary or ASCII file formats with extensions as (.usd), (.usda), (.usdc), and (.usdz). Currently the USD version 0.18.9 is being used in USD COMP. You can drag-drop a USD file into a TouchDesigner network, or import it via the USD File parameter. See also File Types. Parameters (101): USD File, Reload File, Use Material, Cameras, Generate Actor COMPs, Merge Geometry, Merge Level, Max Wired Children, Compute Normals, Direct to GPU, Build Network, Keep Parameters, Keep Connections, Update, Callbacks DAT, Shift Animation Start, Sample Rate Mode, File FPS, Sample Rate, Play Mode, Locked to Timeline, Initialize, Start, Cue.

title: Vertex SOP
text: Vertex SOP (SOP). The Vertex SOP allows you to edit/create attributes on a per-vertex (rather than per-point) basis. It is similar to the Point SOP in this respect. It supports two inputs, and will inherit the first input source by default.  If the second input have less primitives than the first input, it will cycle through the primitives and match the vertices.  If the primitive in the first input has more vertices than the matching primitive in the second input, the extra vertices are ignored. Parameters (13): Group, Color, Keep Color, Alpha, Texture, Keep Texture, Crease, Keep Crease, Custom Attribute, Name, Type, float, Value.

title: Video Device In TOP
text: Video Device In TOP (TOP). The Video Device In TOP can be used to capture video from an external camera, capture card, capture dongle, IP camera, or video decoder connected to the system. Multiple devices can simultaneously stream video into TouchDesigner by using multiple Device In TOPs. HD-SDI video can be streamed into TouchDesigner through capture cards such and those from Blackmagic Design, AJA and Deltacast. Parameters (64): Active, Driver, DirectShow (WDM), Device, NDI Webcam Video 1, Specify IP, IP, Options, Deinterlace, Off, Field Precedence, Even, TV Channel, Signal Format, Quad Link, Input Pixel Format, 8-bit, Transfer Mode, Automatic, Memory Mode, Sync Inputs, Sync Group Index, Max Sync Offset (ms), Sync Timeout (ms).

title: Video Device Out TOP
text: Video Device Out TOP (TOP). The Video Device Out TOP routes video to output devices using their native driver libraries. Parameters (40): Active, Library, Blackmagic, Device, DeckLink 8K Pro (1), Signal Format, Output Pixel Format, 8-bit RGB, Output Color Space, Audio CHOP, Reference Source, Default, Buffer Length, Audio Bit Depth, 16-Bit, Manual Field Control, First Field, Timecode Object/CHOP/DAT, Transfer Mode, Automatic, Sync Outputs, Sync Group Index, Reset Stats, Output Resolution.

title: Video Devices DAT
text: Video Devices DAT (DAT). The Video Devices DAT provides information about all detected video devices including driver, direction (input or output), label, and name that can be referenced in the Video Device In TOP and Video Device Out TOP. Parameters (12): Driver, DirectShow (WDM), All Drivers, Input Devices, Output Devices, Callbacks DAT, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: Video Stream In TOP
text: Video Stream In TOP (TOP). The Video Stream In TOP creates a client to receive video and audio across the network from RTSP, HLS, or SRT sources; or from a WebRTC peer via a WebRTC DAT. Parameters (38): Active, Mode, Server, Server URL, Reload, Reload Pulse, Play, Deinterlace, Off, Field Precedence, Even, Bottom Half is Alpha (AAA), Pre-Read Frames, Max Decode CPUs, Network Buffer Size (KB), Network Queue Size (4KB Each), Disable Buffering, Hardware Decode, WebRTC DAT, WebRTC Connection, WebRTC Track, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution.

title: Video Stream Out TOP
text: Video Stream Out TOP (TOP). Note: This TOP uses the Nvidia Hardware Encoder to create the stream and therefore requires an Nvidia GPU and Windows to operate. Parameters (53): Active, Mode, RTSP Server, Network Port, Stream Name, Multi-Cast, RTMP Destination URL, Destination URL, Force IDR, FPS, Video Codec, H.264 (NVIDIA GPU), Profile, Baseline, Quality, Low-Latency Low, Keyframe Interval, Max B-Frames, Intra-Refresh Period, Intra-Refresh Length, Bitrate Mode, Constant (CBR), Average Bitrate (Mb/s), Per-Frame Metadata.

title: Vioso TOP
text: Vioso TOP (TOP). License: Only available in TouchDesigner Pro. Parameters (19): Configuration File, Projector Index, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu, Use Global Res Multiplier, Output Aspect, Aspect, Aspect1, Aspect Menu, Input Smoothness, Nearest Pixel, Fill Viewer, Viewer Smoothness, Passes, Channel Mask, Pixel Format.

title: Warp CHOP
text: Warp CHOP (CHOP). The Warp CHOP time-warps the channels of the first input (the Pre-Warp Channels) using one warping channel in the second input (the Warp Curve). The Warp Curve acts as either a rate control or an index control, as explained below. The Warp CHOP usually works on non-time-sliced channels, like pre-key-framed channels of motion. See also the Lookup CHOP, the Speed CHOP and the Audio Oscillator CHOP. Parameters (11): Method, Rate Control, Stretch Indices to Channel Length, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: Wave CHOP
text: Wave CHOP (CHOP). The Wave CHOP makes repeating waves with a variety of shapes. It is by default 10-seconds of 1-second sine waves, a total of 600 frames. You can adjust period (frequency), phase, shape, amplitude and offset. Parameters (31): Type, Constant, Period, Period Unit, Phase, Bias, Offset, Amplitude, Decay Rate, Decay Rate Unit, Ramp Slope, Ramp Slope Unit, Expression, Channel Names, Start, Start Unit, End, End Unit, Sample Rate, Extend Left, Hold, Extend Right, Default Value, Time Slice.

title: Web Client DAT
text: Web Client DAT (DAT). The Web Client DAT allows you to send HTTP requests to web servers from TouchDesigner. It supports GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS and PATCH http methods. Parameters (31): Active, Request Method, GET, URL, Upload File, Request, Stop, Stream, Verify Certificate (SSL), Timeout, Include Header in Output, Authentication Type, None, Username, Password, App Key, App Secret, User OAuth Token, User OAuth Secret, Client ID, Token, Clear Output, Clamp Output as Rows, Maximum Lines.

title: Web Render TOP
text: Web Render TOP (TOP). The Web Render TOP takes a URL or DAT and renders a webpage via a separate browser process that uses Chromium Embedded Frameworks, and passes the result back through shared memory. It renders non-Flash pages, including HTML, HTML5, PDF, SVG and more. Targets can be on the internet, in local files with a file:// syntax, or a DAT. Parameters (36): Active, Source, URL or File, DAT, Reload Source, Reload Current Page, Reset Update Count, Only Update when Loaded, Cook Always, Transparent Background, Audio Options, Off, Enable Media Stream, Maximum Render Frame Rate, Number of Frame Buffers, User Cache Directory, Options, Restart if Process Died, Restart, Output Resolution, Use Input, Resolution, W, Resolution Menu.

title: Web Server DAT
text: Web Server DAT (DAT). The Web Server DAT allows you to connect to TouchDesigner as a web client, through a web browser for example. The Web Server DAT supports HTTP, WebSockets, as well as the sending/receiving of binary data, such as images (uploading/downloading). How client requests are handled is left up to the user via callbacks. Parameters (14): Active, Restart, Port, Secure (TLS), Private Key File Path, Certificate File Path, Certificate Password, Callbacks DAT, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: WebRTC DAT
text: WebRTC DAT (DAT). A WebRTC DAT represents a peer on one end of any number of WebRTC peer-to-peer connections. Parameters (17): Active, Reset, Custom Bit Rate Limits, Minimum Bit Rate (kbps), Maximum Bit Rate (kbps), Callbacks DAT, STUN Server URL, TURN Username, TURN Password, TURN Server, TURN Server URL 0, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: WebSocket DAT
text:
WebSocket DAT (DAT). The WebSocket DAT receives and parses WebSocket messages. WebSockets are fast an efficient two way communication protocol used by web servers and clients. Each message is parsed and appended as a row in the DAT's table. The table is FIFO "first-in first-out" and limited to parameter-set number of lines. An optional script may be run for each packet received. Secure (tls) websocket servers are also supported.		
Connections to different WebSocket sites are supported without requiring manual header setup. Parameters (18): Active, Network Address, Network Port, Connection Timeout, Callbacks DAT, Execute from, Current Node, From Operator, Clamp Output, Maximum Lines, Clear Output, Bytes Column, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension, Word Wrap.

title: Widget COMP
text: Widget COMP (COMP). widgetCOMP_Class Parameters (133): X, Y, Width, Height, Fixed Aspect, Off, Aspect Ratio, Depth Layer, Horizontal Mode, Fixed Width, Left Anchor, Left Offset, Right Anchor, Right Offset, Horizontal Origin, Horizontal Fill Weight, Vertical Mode, Fixed Height, Bottom Anchor, Bottom Offset, Top Anchor, Top Offset, Vertical Origin, Vertical Fill Weight.

title: Window COMP
text: Window COMP (COMP). The Window Component allows you to create and maintain a separate floating or fixed window displaying the contents of any Panel or any Node Viewer. Parameters (64): Window Operator, Title, Justisy and Offset to..., Primary Monitor, Ignore Taskbar, Monitor, Justify Horizontal, Left, Justify Vertical, Top, Offset, X, Shift to Single Monitor, DPI Scaling, Native, Opening Size, Automatic from Panel COMP/TOP, Width, Height, Update Settings from Window, Borders, Include Borders in Size, Always on Top, Cursor Visible.

title: Wireframe MAT
text: Wireframe MAT (MAT). The Wireframe MAT renders the edges of polygons and curves as lines. Parameters (45): Color, Red, Alpha, Wireframe Mode, OpenGL Tessellated Wire Frame, Line Width, Deform, Get Bone Data:, From a SOP, SOP with Capture Data, pCaptPath Attrib, pCaptData Attrib, Skeleton Root Path, MAT, Blending (Transparency), Blend Operation, Add, Source Color *, Zero, Destination Color *, One, Separate Alpha Function, Alpha Blend Operation, Source Alpha *.

title: Wireframe SOP
text: Wireframe SOP (SOP). The Wireframe SOP converts edges to tubes and points to spheres, creating the look of a wire frame structure in renderings. This is ideal for modelling tube structures and pipes. Parameters (6): Group, Wire Radius, Round Corners, End Caps, Remove Polygons, Fast Wire.

title: WrnchAI CHOP
text: WrnchAI CHOP (CHOP). License: Only available in TouchDesigner Pro. Parameters (22): wrnchAI License, Model Folder, GPU Device, Default, TOP, Body 3D, Body 2D, Body 3D IK, Face Bounds, Face, Hands Bounds, Hands, Max Players, Aspect Correct UVs, Time Slice, Scope, Sample Rate Match, Resample At First Input's Rate, Export Method, DAT Table by Index, Export Root, Export Table.

title: XML DAT
text: XML DAT (DAT). The XML DAT can be used to parse arbitrary XML and SGML/HTML formatted data. Once formatted, selected sections of the text can be output for further processing. Parameters (25): Parse SGML/HTML, Merge, Before Element, Label, Type, Text, Name, Value, Parent Label, Parent Type, Parent Text, Parent Name, Parent Value, Name Attributes, Value Attributes, Children Labels, Show, Summary Table, Label Prefix, Language, Input, Edit/View Extension, dat, Custom Extension.

title: ZED TOP
